title: Full-Time UI/UX Designer Contractor Kingdom
description:
About Contractor Kingdom

Contractor Kingdom is a digital marketing agency that builds custom websites and runs local SEO campaigns for home service businesses across the United States, HVAC companies, plumbers, roofers, electricians, and more. Our clients trust us to make them look professional online, and design quality is at the heart of what we deliver.

We're a smaller, 3 hands on founders  with a tight-knit remote team with that takes pride in the work we put out. If you're looking for a place where your design skills directly shape the final product clients see, this is it. 



The Role

We're hiring a full-time UI/UX Designer to create custom website page designs in Figma, working under our Head Designer. This is not a template-tweaking job. You'll be designing unique pages, About Us pages, service pages, special offer pages, maintenance plan pages, and more following provided content, for real businesses that rely on these sites to win customers.



Your primary focus:



Design custom website pages in Figma based on direction from our Head Designer

Take a client's brand identity and expand it into fully fleshed-out page designs following provided media & content — custom bullet point icons, unique section layouts, consistent visual language pulled from the homepage inspiration

Deliver polished, developer-ready Figma comps that our Dev WordPress build team can execute faithfully



When you're not designing website pages, you're our go-to creative problem solver:



Reshape and clean up client logos when source files aren't available (this happens a lot)

Create favicons, and thumbnails

Design static ad creatives for client campaigns

Build internal brand assets for Contractor Kingdom

Handle any other visual/design tasks that come across your desk, jack of all trades mentality

Bonus if you have 3D video rendering skills 





What You Must Bring



Strong experience with Figma (this is your primary tool, day in and day out)

Proficiency in Photoshop for image editing, logo work, images and asset creation

Experience using AI tools to enhance your design workflow, without bloating page weight or sacrificing performance

An eye for taking a brand's existing identity and fleshing it out into a full set of cohesive assets (icons, section designs, visual patterns)

Pioneer when it comes to finding cutting edge tools/ software's that can increase our quality & improve your efficiency 

A portfolio or sample work you can share with your application (required)

Reliable internet and a setup that supports full-time remote work





Who Thrives in This Role

You're someone who is hungry. You want to get better at design, not just clock hours. You take feedback well, you study what good design looks like, and you're always looking for ways to level up your craft. You don't need to be hand-held, give you a brand direction and reference material/ websites , and you can run with it.

You're comfortable being a generalist when the team needs it. One hour you're designing custom pages, the next you're exporting a favicon. That responsibility excites you, not overwhelms you.



What We Offer



$1,000 USD/month starting salary

We cover software expenses 

Flexible, async-friendly schedule (40 hours/week, manage your own time)

Regular performance reviews with honest, constructive feedback & client alignment! 

Clear path for growth as you prove yourself and the agency scales

Direct collaboration with our Head Designer, PM, President you'll learn and grow & level up the design game alongside experienced leadership

A team that respects good work and treats people well

title: Amazon Catalog Coordinator
description:
Amazon Catalog Coordinator

Up to $1,600 Monthly + Benefits



We are looking for an Amazon Catalog Coordinator to help manage catalogs on 
. We are looking for someone who understands the importance of optimized product detail pages and how to help us win the digital shelf through best-in-class product content. The successful candidate will spend most of their time within the 
 Seller Central or Vendor Central portal, ensuring digital merchandising strategy is best in class. We are looking for someone with strong attention to detail and firm knowledge of how to create new product listings and variation families, upload digital content, and audit detail pages for accuracy. If you are excited about working in a fast-moving space with an opportunity to expand skills on Amazon as well as across other ecommerce platforms, this is the perfect job for you.



Responsibilities

*Work with client success managers (CSMs) and account directors (ADs) to implement the product detail page changes for clients on Amazon.

*Communicate directly with the team on tasks related to catalog optimization efforts.

*Monitor product sales and trends using the proprietary sales and reporting dashboard.

*Collect product data and organize it into the product management software.

*Create, optimize, and upload listing content—titles, bullet points, and descriptions.

*Upload enhanced digital assets created by the internal creative team—A+ content, videos, images, and brand stores.

*Run product page diagnostics on existing products on Amazon to determine the accuracy of the content.

*Conduct product and competitor research to aid in executing an effective listing strategy.

*Monitor both account and listing health closely to limit listing and account downtime.

*Report product pages that fraudulently use the clients' trademarks or copyrighted material.

*Respond to buyer messages in a timely manner while providing excellent customer service.

*Maintain current knowledge of Amazon's policies and best practices for product listings.

*Other responsibilities that come when working with a growing agency.



Successful Candidates

*2–5+ years’ experience working with and on 
 (Vendor Central and Seller Central).

*Proficient in both Seller Central and Vendor Central, with a primary focus on the Vendor Central catalog.

*Bonus points for experience working on international catalogs in Vendor Central.

*Knowledge of the Amazon back-end and the ability to load new products, create variations, fix suppressed listings, upload creative content, and do other similar work within the Amazon portal.

*An extreme eye for detail with impeccable organization skills.

*Proficient multitasker with the ability to manage many tasks at once.

*Ability to work in a collaborative environment.

*A problem solver who can work with the many challenges and inconsistencies the Amazon platform provides.

*English speaking—an effective and timely communicator.

*Proficient in Microsoft Excel.

*Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, or a related field is preferred.



Benefits

*3 weeks of PTO

*10 holidays

*2 personal days

title: Graphic Artist Photoshop & Illustrator Specialist

title: Technical Operations Manager (Virtual)
description:
Role Overview



We are seeking a high-agency Technical Operations Manager to serve as the "right hand" to leadership. This is not a traditional administrative role; it is a hybrid position requiring a blend of executive support, sales development, multimedia production, and software automation.



The AI Philosophy: We expect you to be an "AI-First" professional. You should be proficient in leveraging LLMs and automation tools to 10x your output. However, AI is your assistant, not your replacement. You must possess the fundamental skills to audit, edit, and improve everything the AI produces to ensure 100% quality and brand alignment.

Key Responsibilities

1. Executive & Operations Support



    Gatekeeping: Manage complex calendars and high-volume
 inboxes with discretion and precision.

    Vendor & Talent Management: Oversee external vendors and qualify applicants for open roles using our established internal processes.

    Process Documentation: Build and maintain "Standard Operating Procedures" (SOPs) to ensure the business scales efficiently.



2. Sales, CRM & Lead Management



    Lead Pipeline: Add, qualify, and segment leads within the CRM.

    Active Outreach: Initiate contact and execute rigorous follow-up sequences to move leads through the funnel.

    Data Integrity: Ensure all communications are logged and the CRM remains the "single source of truth."



3. Multimedia & Content Production



    Podcast Management: Edit raw audio/video recordings into polished episodes (including intro/outros, noise reduction, and flow).

    Publishing: Manage the end-to-end distribution process across hosting platforms and social media.

    Social Content: Slicing long-form content into high-engagement clips for various social channels.



4. Automation & Technical Projects



    No-Code/Low-Code Dev: Build and maintain automations (Zapier, Make, or similar) to connect our tech stack.

    Coding: Assist with software-based coding projects (Python, JavaScript, or similar) to solve specific business problems.

    AI Implementation: Identify opportunities to integrate AI into existing workflows to increase efficiency.



Qualifications & Skills



    The Fundamentals: Exceptional written and verbal communication. You must be able to write a professional
 and a technical brief without relying on "Generic AI-speak."

    Technical Stack: Proficiency in CRM software (HubSpot, Salesforce, or Pipedrive), Podcast Editing software (Descript, Adobe Audition, or similar), and project management tools (ClickUp, Notion, or Asana).

    Coding Literacy: Ability to read, write, and debug code for business automation and software projects.

    AI Mastery: Expert-level prompting skills and experience with AI-assisted coding and content generation.

    The "Vibe": You are a self-starter who solves problems before they reach the CEO's desk.



The Hiring Process (Our "Standard of Excellence")



We value your time and ours. Our vetting process is rigorous:



    Initial Screen: Review of portfolio and technical experience.

    The "Quality Test": A paid work sample involving a complex automation task and a communication scenario where you must audit an AI-generated response for quality.

    Video Interview: A deep dive into your logic, technical stack, and cultural fit.



Why This Role?



You will have the autonomy to build systems from the ground up and work at the bleeding edge of AI and business operations. You won't just be "checking boxes"—you will be driving the engine.



Application Process



To be considered for this position, please visit: 



This position requires candidates to successfully complete our comprehensive evaluation process, which includes technical assessments and multiple interview stages to ensure the best fit for both the role and our organizational culture.

title: Elite Cold Email Copywriter for B2B Lead Gen Campaigns (Apollo / Instantly / Clay)
description:
Job Description



We are looking for an elite B2B cold
 copywriter who knows how to write messages that actually get opened, read, replied to, and booked.



This is not a generic copywriting role. We need someone who understands cold outbound, knows how to write for different buyer types, and can help create high-performing
 copy for highly targeted lead generation campaigns.



You will be helping us write and improve cold
 campaigns for 4 niches:



Alternative Lending / Business Financing



Property Management



Real Estate Agents / Realtors



Dentists



We use tools like Apollo, Instantly, and Clay, so experience writing for personalized outbound systems is very important.



We are looking for someone sharp, strategic, fast, and obsessed with performance.



What You’ll Be Doing



Writing cold
 sequences for outbound B2B campaigns



Creating copy for different niches, offers, and buyer personas



Writing short, punchy, high-reply-rate
s



Adapting messaging based on industry pain points and market awareness



Creating variations for A/B testing



Writing subject lines, first lines, CTA angles, and follow-ups



Collaborating on campaign strategy, hooks, and positioning



Using prospect/company data from Clay or Apollo to support personalized messaging



Improving campaigns based on reply rates, positive responses, and booked meetings



What We Need From You



Proven experience writing cold
 copy for B2B outbound



Strong understanding of what makes someone reply to a cold



Ability to write in a natural, human, non-spammy tone



Ability to switch tone and messaging depending on niche



Good understanding of offers, pain points, objections, and direct response principles



Experience writing copy for lead gen agencies, appointment setting, SaaS, or service businesses is a big plus



Experience with Apollo, Instantly, Clay, Smartlead, or similar tools is a strong plus



Strong English writing skills



Ability to think strategically, not just “write nicely”



You’re a Strong Fit If



You understand the difference between bad cold
 and high-converting cold



You know how to make
s feel relevant without sounding over-personalized or fake



You can write copy that is concise, confident, and response-driven



You understand how to test hooks, angles, and call-to-actions



You can take rough ideas and turn them into polished outbound campaigns



Nice to Have



Experience writing for any of these niches:



Alternative lending



Real estate



Property management



Dental



Experience analyzing campaign performance and suggesting improvements



Experience writing both plain-text cold
s and LinkedIn outreach copy



Familiarity with deliverability best practices



To Apply



Please send:



A short introduction about yourself



Examples of cold
 campaigns you’ve written



The niches you’ve written for before



Any results you’ve helped generate (reply rates, booked calls, etc.)



Your rate



Your answer to this question:

What do you think makes a cold
 feel relevant without feeling fake or over-personalized?



Important



We are not looking for generic marketing copy, blog writing, or social media writing.



We want someone who understands outbound, buyer psychology, and reply-driven cold
 copy.



If you are the kind of copywriter who can help build campaigns that generate real conversations and real revenue, we want to hear from you.

title: Facilities Dispatch & Administrative Coordinator
description:
Position Summary



The Facilities Dispatch & Administrative Coordinator is responsible for the end-to-end management of facility maintenance work orders within the Axxerion platform. This role ensures all work orders are properly scheduled, assigned to vendors, tracked through completion, and processed for billing and invoicing. The coordinator serves as the central point of contact between District Managers, Store Managers, vendors, and the IPG Accounts Payable team.

Key Responsibilities



??????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????



Work Order Management & Dispatch



Create, assign, and schedule work orders in Axxerion across the IPG property portfolio

Coordinate with vendors to confirm start dates and ensure timely execution

Monitor work order statuses (Draft, Assigned, Accepted, Work Finished) and follow up on stalled or overdue items

Triage incoming maintenance requests by priority level (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and route accordingly

Workflow Coordination

Manage the 4-phase work order lifecycle:

Phase 1 — Assignment & vendor confirmation

Phase 2 — Work execution tracking & District Manager approval

Phase 3 — Financial confirmation & NTE (Not-to-Exceed) compliance

Phase 4 — Change order submissions & approval routing

Ensure
 notifications are triggering properly across the 10 workflow connectors

Escalate rejected work or change orders and coordinate resubmissions

Billing & Invoicing

Verify vendor invoices against approved NTE amounts and work order scope

Reconcile spend amounts with work order budgets before submitting to IPG Accounts Payable

Track and flag discrepancies between quoted and actual costs

Process change orders through the approval chain (including escalation to senior leadership as needed)

Maintain accurate financial records for each work order

Reporting & Communication

Utilize the FM Master Dashboard to monitor KPIs, open work orders, and regional performance

Provide regular status updates to District Managers and Regional Managers

Generate reports on work order volume, vendor performance, completion rates, and spend by property/region

Maintain organized records and documentation for all maintenance activities

Vendor Relations

Serve as primary contact for vendor scheduling and communications

Track vendor responsiveness, quality, and adherence to timelines

Assist in onboarding new vendors into the Axxerion system



??????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????



Qualifications



2+ years experience in facilities coordination, dispatch, property management, or administrative operations

Experience with work order management systems (Axxerion or similar CMMS/CAFM platforms preferred)

Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage high volumes of concurrent work orders

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and comfort navigating web-based dashboards

Familiarity with invoice processing, purchase orders, and basic accounting principles

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Self-storage or commercial real estate experience a plus



??????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????



Competencies



Detail-oriented with strong follow-through

Ability to prioritize and triage under pressure (especially critical/emergency work orders)

Comfortable coordinating across multiple stakeholders (store-level, regional, vendor, and corporate)

Proactive problem-solver who escalates appropriately

description:
Location: Remote

About the Role: The Chiropractic Freedom Coalition is seeking a high-level AI Specialist to help us complete our professional tech stack. We are not looking for someone who simply "uses" ChatGPT; we need a builder who can take conceptual "bones" and turn them into high-quality, professional-grade applications and automated systems for a growing organization.



Key Responsibilities:



Develop and deploy professional apps and tools to streamline organizational communication and workflows.

Build out "airtight" information systems, specifically focusing on integrated
 and text communication stacks.

Take existing skeletal systems and scale them into fully operational, business-ready solutions.

Proven Building Experience: You must have a portfolio of high-quality, professional apps built for businesses or organizations.

Technical Proficiency: Deep experience in Replit is ideally required. You should be comfortable building and deploying within this environment.

Beyond Prompting: You should be an expert in the backend of AI implementation, not just front-end prompting.

Strategic Execution: Ability to take a high-level strategic idea and execute the technical requirements independently.





Project Details: This position will start as project-based with the potential to move into a consistent part-time role. We are looking for someone to help us "complete the tech stack" ASAP so we can move forward with broader enrollment and hiring goals. Our offer is starting at $10/hour (Negotiable based on portfolio and Replit proficiency).



Application Requirements

Portfolio of Live Apps: Links to at least 2-3 professional applications or automated systems you have built for businesses. (Please specify your role in the development of each).

Replit Profile or GitHub Link: Direct evidence of your coding environment and ability to build/deploy "stuff" (as Steve requested).

Specific Tool Stack: A brief list of the AI models (e.g., GPT-4o, Claude 3.5), APIs, and integration tools (e.g., Zapier, Make, Pinecone) you are most proficient with.

title: Facility Monitoring & Response Specialist
description:
MISSION STATEMENT



SecureSpace Self Storage is one of the fastest-growing Self Storage platforms in the USA, with assets located primarily in high-density, urban markets. With a focus on exceptional customer service and upgraded security features, SecureSpace provides a high-quality experience that our customers can count on in any store they visit.



SCOPE OF POSITION



The Customer Service Security Support Representative at SecureSpace is responsible for maintaining facility safety and operational integrity through proactive monitoring, rapid i
 response, and exceptional customer interaction. This remote role requires a vigilant, detail-oriented individual who can assess real-time security situations, engage with law enforcement or internal teams when needed, and provide accurate documentation post-i
. With access to advanced systems such as PTI, OpenTech, Spot AI, and Turing, this tea
mber plays a crucial role in safeguarding our nationwide facilities and ensuring a seamless customer experience.



This position requires strong critical thinking, situational awareness, and communication skills, as well as a willingness to step into fast-moving situations, provide clarity, and document everything from first alert to after-action reporting.



SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:



· 2+ years in customer service, security operations, or dispatch coordination.



· High school diploma or equivalent required; college education is a plus.



· Comfortable navigating surveillance and facility management systems (PTI, OpenTech, Spot AI, Turing).



· Strong verbal communication with ability to calmly engage with law enforcement and internal teams.



· Highly organized, analytical, and able to track movement across multiple data sources.



· Self-driven, dependable, and flexible to shift between security monitoring and customer support roles.



· Experience handling confidential or sensitive information with discretion.



RESPONSIBILITIES: include (but are not limited to):



· Respond to alerts involving open gates, suspicious activity, or reported break-ins.



· Call local Police Departments (non-emergency line) when needed and guide responding officers through the site.



· Gather critical details for i
 resolution: determine if individuals involved are tenants, verify site hours, confirm if "Do Not Trespass" orders are in place, and identify how the i
 began.



· Escalate i
 to the Area Manager when necessary; follow a clear escalation path if unreachable.



· Collect and share evidence (photos, videos, system logs) with PD or internal stakeholders as needed.



· Track individuals across the site using available tools, determine current presence or departure, and provide full traceable timelines.



· Prepare and distribute After-Action Reports via
 to key stakeholders 



· Support live i
 response in real time, participate in post-event recaps, and continuously refine response protocols.



· Perform additional duties as assigned, especially in response to evolving security risks.



REPORTS TO: Security Operations Lead / Customer Success Manager



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:



Qualified individuals must be able to perform the position's essential duties with or without accommodation. A qualified person with a disability may request a modification or adjustment to the job or work environment to meet the physical requirements of the position. GSA/SecureSpace will attempt to satisfy requests if the accommodations needed are reasonable and no undue hardship would result.



· Ability to perform essential job functions while seated for extended periods, including working on a computer and taking phone calls, as well as sit, walk, stand, bend, squat, climb, kneel, and twist intermittently or continuously.



· Must be capable of reviewing surveillance footage, reading i
 reports, and compiling documentation with or without visual aids.



· Capability to read documents and focus on computer screens, with or without corrective lenses as may be required.



· Ability to arrange the workspace efficiently for productivity, accommodating any individual needs for ergonomic considerations.



· Willingness to transition between various tasks and responsibilities while maintaining work efficiency and effectiveness.

title: Doc Drawer (US Mortgage Company | Full-Time | Night Shift | Remote)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



Work Setup: 100% Remote (Work From Home)

Schedule: Monday to Friday | Night Shift (U.S. Pacific Time)

Employment Type: Full-Time | Long-Term Position



About the Role



We are a U.S.-based mortgage company looking for a Doc Drawer to join our growing operations team.



Your main responsibility is to prepare and generate accurate loan documents before closing. This is a critical position — errors in documentation can delay loan closings, so we are looking for someone who is highly detail-oriented and reliable.



If you are organized, focused, and serious about building a long-term career in the U.S. mortgage industry, this role is for you.



Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. Applicants with 1–2 years of U.S. mortgage experience are strongly preferred.

> Verify borrower information, loan terms, fees, and rates

> Coordinate with processors, underwriters, and closing team

> Ensure loan packages are complete before release



Qualifications

> Good written English communication

What We Offer

title: Senior Non-QM Mortgage Underwriter (Remote | Night Shift)
description:
Senior Non-QM Mortgage Underwriter (Remote | Night Shift)

Php 70,000 – Php 88,179/month (based on experience)

Full-Time | Permanent Remote | U.S. Mortgage Company



>Why This Role is Different



We’re not looking for a basic underwriter.



We’re hiring someone who can confidently review and approve complex Non-QM loans — someone who understands risk, moves fast, and knows how to make solid credit decisions.



If you have strong Non-QM experience and want to work directly with a growing U.S.-based mortgage team, this is a long-term opportunity with real impact.



> What You’ll Be Doing



Underwrite Non-QM loans (DSCR, Bank Statement, WVOE, P&L)



Analyze credit, income, assets, and collateral



Review AUS findings and apply conditions or overlays



Issue decisions: Approve, Deny, or Suspend



Work closely with processors and operations to clear conditions



Maintain strong turn times while ensuring compliance



Support junior tea
mbers when needed



> What We’re Looking For



At least 5 years U.S. mortgage underwriting experience



Strong Non-QM experience (DSCR, Bank Statement, etc.)



Experience handling self-employed borrowers and complex files



Solid understanding of U.S. mortgage guidelines (TRID, ATR/QM, etc.)



Able to make independent credit decisions



Detail-oriented and reliable in a remote setup



Plus (but not required):



Experience with Encompass



Korean, Chinese, or Vietnamese language skills



> Work Setup



Remote (Work from Home)



Night Shift: 11:30 PM – 8:30 AM PH Time



Stable, long-term role with a U.S.-based company



> Basic Requirements



At least 8GB RAM



Intel i5 (4th gen or newer) or equivalent



Stable internet (minimum 100 Mbps)



>> How to Apply

title: Remote Mortgage Funder
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



Job Title: Remote Mortgage Funder

Location: Remote

Job Type: Full-time

Experience Level: Entry-Level to Junior (1 year experience REQUIRED)



Job Overview



We are seeking a Remote Mortgage Funder to join our dynamic team. While fresh graduates are welcome, at least 1 year of related mortgage funding or closing experience is preferred. This role is ideal for candidates who are eager to learn and grow within the U.S. mortgage industry and develop expertise in mortgage procedures, funding requirements, and regulatory compliance.



The Mortgage Funder will be responsible for ensuring the timely and accurate funding of mortgage loans while working closely with underwriters, loan officers, processors, and closing agents to support smooth and efficient loan transactions.



Key Responsibilities

> Loan Funding

> Review and verify loan documents to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance with company policies and regulatory guidelines

> Prepare and execute funding wires and disbursement checks

> Coordinate with title companies and escrow agents to confirm all conditions are met prior to loan disbursement

> Ensure all required documentation is received, reviewed, and properly filed



Quality Control

> Conduct final audits of closed loan packages to ensure compliance with federal and state regulations

> Identify and resolve discrepancies or funding issues in a timely manner

> Maintain accurate and organized records of all funding transactions

> Communication and Collaboration

> Work closely with underwriters, loan officers, processors, and closing agents to facilitate efficient loan closings

> Provide professional and timely support to internal and external stakeholders

> Communicate funding delays, issues, or requirements clearly to all relevant parties



Compliance and Risk Management

> Stay current with industry regulations, investor guidelines, and company policies

> Participate in training and development programs to continuously improve knowledge and performance

> Assist with improving funding procedures and best practices as needed



Qualifications

> Education

> High school diploma or equivalent required

> Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, or a related field is preferred



Experience

> Fresh graduates are welcome

> At least 1 year of related mortgage experience (funder, closer, post-closing, or similar role) is preferred

> Familiarity with U.S. mortgage lending processes and documentation is a plus



Skills and Abilities

> Strong attention to detail and accuracy

> Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills

> Clear written and verbal communication skills

> Ability to work independently in a remote environment

> Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook); experience with mortgage loan systems is a plus



Compensation and Benefits

> Competitive salary based on experience

> Opportunities for professional growth and career advancement

> Training and exposure to U.S. mortgage operations



Work Setup



Location: Remote

Schedule: 11:30 pm to 8:30 am PH time



Compensation: Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications.



Minimum Technical Requirements (IMPORTANT)



Applicants must meet the following minimum requirements:



1. Computer Requirements



At least 8GB RAM

Intel Core i5 4th Generation or newer (or equivalent AMD processor)

Reliable computer capable of handling mortgage systems and multiple documents



2. Internet Requirements



Stable internet connection

Minimum 100 Mbps download speed

 their application to BOTH
 addresses below:

 Must Include ALL of the Following

title: Inside Sales Representative (Setter to Closer Track)
description:
(We're not a VA Agency.)

Salary: Starting at $800–$1,000/month USD + Uncapped Commissions (2% per sale)

Location: Work from Home (Philippines)

Hours: Full-Time, Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Central U.S. Time (Night Shift PH)

On-Target Earnings: $1,500–$2,000+ USD/month for high performers



About the Role

BridgeCom Systems is hiring a driven and coachable Inside Sales Representative to join our growing global team. You’ll be working from home, helping warm leads and interested customers learn about our plug-and-play radio solutions.

This role will start as a Setter position (booking appointments, qualifying leads, and moving opportunities forward), but top performers can grow into a Closer role responsible for handling full sales calls and closing deals.



This is not a call center job. You’ll be representing a real U.S. company with real customers and building real relationships. Your job will be to connect, follow up, and close sales over the phone,
, and text using our scripts and proven system.

You’ll receive world-class sales training through Cardone University and daily mentorship. If you’re hungry, coachable, and ready to change your life, this is the career for you.



About BridgeCom Systems

We’re a U.S.-based communications company that’s been in business for over 20 years. We help radio operators stay connected with powerful, easy-to-use radio systems.

We’re a small, friendly, and values-driven team that believes in hard work, ownership, and opportunity. Many of our Filipino tea
mbers have been with us for years—we invest in our people and treat them like family.



Key Responsibilities

Call and follow up with warm leads and existing prospects

Set appointments, qualify prospects, and move deals through the pipeline (Setter responsibilities)

Use scripts to guide conversations and close sales (once promoted into a Closer track)

Send quotes, follow up by text/email, and update the CRM (HubSpot)

Learn and speak confidently about our radio products (training provided)

Log your activities and hit daily targets (calls, appointments, follow-ups, and sales)

Work closely with U.S. teammates for support and coaching



Requirements

Excellent spoken and written English

Sales experience preferred (2-3  year outbound/inbound)

Friendly, confident voice and good phone presence

High-speed internet (min. 10 Mbps) + backup power

Quiet work environment, noise-canceling headset

Coachable, self-motivated, and goal-oriented

Familiarity with CRM tools (HubSpot a plus)

Tech-savvy and comfortable using web tools and scripts

Willingness to learn about Ham Radio and our industry

Experienced in radio communications systems and technical support



Tools & Setup

You must have:

A fast, reliable wired internet connection

Backup power (UPS or generator preferred)

Quiet workspace free from distractions

A working headset with clear audio

A Windows or Mac computer capable of running multiple apps



How to Apply

To apply,
 your resume and a 30-second voice or video recording introducing yourself and telling us why you’re a great fit to: 

Subject line: Inside Sales – [Your Name] – Found on OnlineJobs.ph

Start your message with: BLUE M&MS

Applications without a video/voice sample WILL NOT BE REVIEWED.



Why Join Us

Work from home with a U.S. company that values Filipino talent

Get paid to train in one of the best sales systems in the world (Cardone U Sales training every day)

Uncapped commissions + stable long-term work

Clear KPIs, daily coaching, and professional growth

Join a growing company with real career opportunities

title: Level 2 IT Support Technician (MSP / Remote)
description:
We are a U.S.-based Managed Service Provider (MSP) seeking an experienced Level 2 IT Support Technician to join our team full-time.



This role is for someone who can go beyond basic helpdesk tasks and take ownership of technical issues, troubleshoot efficiently, and provide excellent customer service.



You will work closely with our dispatcher and clients to resolve tickets, support end users, and maintain stable IT environments.



Key Responsibilities:

• Troubleshoot and resolve Level 1 and Level 2 support tickets

• Provide remote support for desktops, laptops, and mobile devices

• Support Microsoft 365 (users,
, permissions, troubleshooting)

• Assist with networking issues (routers, firewalls, Wi-Fi)

• Manage and maintain Windows environments

• Document work clearly in the ticketing system

• Communicate professionally with clients

• Escalate complex issues when necessary



Requirements:

• Strong English communication (written and spoken)

• 3+ years of IT support experience (MSP experience preferred)

• Experience with Microsoft 365 administration

• Good understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, VPN)

• Experience with remote support tools and ticketing systems

• Ability to work during U.S. business hours

• Reliable internet and quiet work environment



Preferred Experience:

• MSP experience

• Experience with RMM tools (Syncro, Ninja, etc.)

• Basic cybersecurity awareness

• Experience documenting processes



IMPORTANT – VIDEO REQUIREMENT:

To be considered, you MUST submit a short video (2–3 minutes) answering the following:



1. Tell us about your IT experience and environments you’ve supported

2. Walk us through how you would troubleshoot a user who cannot access

3. What hours are you available to work (local time and U.S. time)

4. Do you currently have any other jobs or clients?



Applications without a video will NOT be considered.



Technical Screening (Required):

Please also include written answers to the following:



1. What is DNS and why is it important?

2. What steps would you take if a computer cannot connect to the internet?

3. What is the difference between local user and domain user?



We are looking for someone long-term who wants to grow with our company.



To confirm you read this job post, include the word "L2TechPro" in your application subject line.

title: Account Manager
description:
About Us 



LecsIT is a growing managed IT services company that helps small and mid-sized businesses stay secure, productive, and confident in their technology. We focus on long-term relationships, proactive support, and clear communication—not ticket factories or one-size-fits-all IT. 



The Role 



We’re looking for an Account Manager who thrives at the intersection of relationships and technology. This role is all about owning client relationships, translating business needs into IT solutions, and making sure customers feel heard, supported, and strategically guided. 



You’ll act as the primary point of contact for assigned clients, working closely with our technical team to ensure service delivery aligns with expectations while also identifying opportunities to improve the client’s IT posture. 



Key Responsibilities 

-Serve as the primary relationship owner for a portfolio of clients 

-Conduct regular check-ins, quarterly business reviews, and strategic planning conversations 

-Translate technical concepts into clear, business-friendly language 

-Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities aligned with client goals (security, compliance, cloud, etc.) 

-Coordinate with internal technical teams to ensure smooth delivery and follow-through 

-Advocate for the client internally while protecting company standards and scope 

-Track client satisfaction and proactively address risks before they become issues 



What Success Looks Like 

-Clients feel confident, informed, and cared for 

-Renewals are smooth and predictable 

-Expansion opportunities are uncovered naturally through trust 

-Clear documentation of client needs, decisions, and next steps 



Qualifications 

-2+ years in account management, customer success, or client-facing roles 

-Experience in IT, MSP, SaaS, or technology services strongly preferred 

-Strong communication and relationship-building skills 

-Comfortable discussing security, compliance, and IT strategy at a high level 

-Organized, proactive, and able to manage multiple accounts effectively 



Bonus Points 

-MSP or IT services experience 

-Familiarity with Microsoft 365, cybersecurity concepts, or compliance frameworks 

-Experience conducting QBRs or executive-level conversations 



What We Offer 

-Competitive salary with performance-based incentives 

-Flexible work environment 

-Opportunity to grow with a fast-moving, relationship-driven IT company 

-Direct impact on client success and company growth



To apply, please provide a short voice introduction via 
.

title: HVAC ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT
description:
HVAC Administrative Assistant (Part-Time – 5

 and basic computer

title: Facebook Media Buyer ($1M+ Monthly Ad Spend)
description:
Remote — Full Time — Experienced



Our Ecommerce brands is one of the fastest-growing eye-health supplement brands in the DTC space. We scale aggressively using high-volume creative testing, fast iteration, and extremely strict ad account systems. We’re looking for a Media Buyer / Ad Launcher who is obsessed with speed, accuracy, and following instructions with zero errors.



This is NOT a traditional “optimization + creative strategy” media buying role.

This is a high-output, operationally-focused ad launcher role where you will:



- Upload 30 videos per day

- Launch 4 testing campaigns per day

- Follow our exact CBO/ABO structure

- Update our tracking systems daily

- Be extremely responsive and detail-oriented



If you are fast, organized, and thrive in an environment where KPIs are everything, you’ll love this.



YOUR MISSION



Your mission is to upload, organize, and launch ads at high volume with perfect accuracy, following our exact campaign structure and daily KPI requirements.



Your speed and precision allow our brand to scale faster, test more angles, and keep the creative engine at full capacity.



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

- Daily Uploading & Tracking

- Upload 30 ads per day into Ads Manager

- Upload every creative to Notion using our exact naming structure

- Update our Google Sheets tracking immediately after each upload

- Ensure every piece of creative is properly tagged, categorized, and ready for launch

- Identify duplicates, missing assets, or incorrect formats before uploading

- Daily Campaign Launching

- Launch 2-4 Testing CBOs per day

- Launch 10–15 ads per Testing CBO (budget $500 each)



- Launch to both Main USA CBO and Testing CBOs as required

- Launch winners into Scaling ABOs ($200 budget)

- Launch all winning ads to our landing pages + advertorial funnels



Campaign Structure Adherence



You must follow our structure EXACTLY:



- Main USA CBO: all evergreen ads tested here

- Advertorial CBO: ads directed to advertorial funnels

- Testing CBO: 10–15 ads per CBO, $500 budget

- Scaling ABO: winning creatives moved here at $200

- Whitelisting pages: upload and test identical creatives under different profiles



Any deviation must be reported immediately.





Performance Alerts



You don’t optimize — you report issues.

You must notify founders within minutes if:



-NC-ROAS drops below 0.8

-CAC rises above $80

-Ads stop serving

-Ad sets get rejected

-CBOs spend unevenly

-Any part of the structure breaks



Daily Communication:



-Fast responses during working hours

-Daily “end-of-day” recap

-Highlight what was uploaded, launched, and any issues

-Follow instructions word-for-word



OUR TECH STACK



You must be proficient with:



Meta Ads Manager

Notion (uploading creatives, organization)

Google Sheets (tracking all uploads & tests)

Slack / WhatsApp (fast communication)



KPIs YOU WILL OWN



These are the KPIs your entire role is judged by:



Daily KPIs

- 30 video uploads per day (non-negotiable)

- 4 Testing CBO launches per day

- All uploads posted to Notion with correct format

- Tracking Sheet updated daily



Performance KPIs

- Alert when NC-ROAS is over 0.85 (scale signal)

- Alert when CAC is over $80

- Zero errors in naming, uploading, or launching



Communication KPIs

- Messages responded to within 5–10 minutes

- Daily EOD update delivered on time

- No missed instructions or overlooked notes



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

- Experience managing $500k+ monthly ad spend

- Experience launching high-volume Meta ads

- Extremely fast worker with high output

- Obsessed with accuracy, systems, and organization



Follows instructions precisely

- Strong English reading comprehension

- Zero ego, zero excuses

- Thrives in fast-paced DTC testing environments

- Capable of handling pressure + rapid changes

- Works reliably, consistently, and quickly



WHY JOIN US

- Work directly with founders of a rapidly scaling DTC supplement brand

- Full autonomy over your uploads + ad launching

- Clear KPIs, no ambiguity

- High-growth environment with constant testing

- No corporate BS — just speed, output, and results

- Your performance is recognized and rewarded

- You get to be part of a brand scaling aggressively month-over-month



THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF

- You are slow to respond

- You miss small details

- You guess instead of asking

- You cannot follow strict instructions

- You prefer creative strategy over operational precision

- You cannot manage high-volume uploads

- You make frequent mistakes or need hand-holding

- You don’t like fast changes or tight KPIs

- You need constant supervision



COMPENSATION

Base Salary: $1,500 per month

KPI Bonus (Option A):

$100–$200 monthly bonus for:



Hitting 100% of daily uploading KPIs:

- Zero mistakes in structure

- On-time launches

- Accurate tracking

- Fast daily responses



Total potential monthly earnings: $1,600–$1,700



(Pay increases available after 60–90 days if KPIs are hit consistently.)



HOW TO APPLY



To apply, send:

- Your experience managing large Meta budgets

- The biggest monthly spend you’ve personally handled

- Your experience with high-volume testing

- Your experience launching 20–50+ ads per day

- Tools you’ve used (Notion, Sheets, Ads Manager)

- Your availability + working hours

- Why you think you’re a great fit for our brand

- Confirm you understand you will NOT create ads — only upload & launch

- Confirm you understand the KPIs listed above



Include the codeword: ReadEveryInstruction



Applications without the codeword will NOT be reviewed.

title: Real Editor
description:
Video Editor Wanted

Looking for a video editor to join a documentary-style channel.

title: HOA Administrative Assistant
description:
ABOUT THIS ROLE



We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive HOA Administrative Assistant to support the day-to-day operations of a growing HOA consulting and community management company. This is a junior-level administrative role ideal for someone with hands-on CINC System experience and a solid understanding of HOA operations.



You will work directly with the operations team to manage community records, process ACC applications, handle homeowner communications, and maintain data accuracy within the CINC platform — ensuring the consulting team can deliver seamless service across multiple communities.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

- CINC System Administration

- Enter and maintain accurate homeowner, community, and vendor data in CINC

- Generate and distribute reports, notices, and correspondence through CINC

- Process payments, track violations, and update community records in CINC

- Troubleshoot basic CINC data discrepancies and escalate as needed



HOA Administrative Support

- Assist community managers with daily operational tasks across multiple HOA accounts

- Prepare and distribute meeting agendas, minutes, and board correspondence

- Track and follow up on homeowner inquiries, requests, and complaints in a timely manner

- Support enforcement workflows including issuing violation notices and tracking corrective actions



ACC (Architectural Control Committee) Support

- Receive, log, and process ACC applications through CINC

- Communicate with homeowners regarding application status, approvals, and required revisions

- Maintain accurate ACC records including approvals, denials, and supporting documentation

- Coordinate with the ACC and community managers to ensure compliance with design standards



Records & Documentation

- Maintain organized digital records of homeowner communications, contracts, and community activity

- Assist with preparation of community reports, compliance summaries, and board updates

- Ensure all documentation is current, accurate, and easily accessible within company systems



Team Collaboration

- Participate in daily/weekly team check-ins and contribute updates on assigned tasks

- Provide cross-functional support to Finance Tea
mbers as needed

- Identify process gaps and suggest improvements to increase team efficiency



QUALIFICATIONS & REQUIREMENTS

Required

- CINC System experience — must be able to demonstrate working knowledge of the platform

- Minimum 1–2 years of experience in HOA administration, property management, or a related field

- Strong attention to detail and data accuracy

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English

- Ability to manage multiple tasks and communities simultaneously

Proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Gmail) and standard office tools



Preferred

- Experience supporting HOA consulting or community management companies (multi-community environment)

- Familiarity with HOA governing documents, covenants, and compliance procedures

- Experience with ACC processing and homeowner correspondence

- Completion of M100: The Essentials of Community Association Management (or willingness to complete within 12 months)



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



The ideal candidate embodies the Driver's Seat Mentality™ — they don't wait to be told what to do next. They take initiative, follow through on commitments, think critically about how their work impacts the broader team, and consistently deliver accurate results without being micromanaged.



Proactive Leadership

- Anticipates needs, takes initiative, and acts without waiting for step-by-step direction.

- Strategic Thinking

- Considers downstream effects of actions and prioritizes tasks effectively across multiple accounts.

- Results Accountability

- Owns their work quality, meets deadlines, and follows through without needing reminders.

- Continuous Innovation

- Looks for smarter ways to handle recurring tasks and suggests improvements proactively.

- Client Advocacy

- Treats every homeowner and board interaction as a reflection of the client's brand.





HOW TO APPLY

If you have CINC System experience and a background in HOA administration, we'd love to hear from you. To be considered, your application must include ALL of the following:



1. Updated Resume — PDF format only

Your resume must be current and submitted as a PDF file. No Word docs or links.



2. Video Introduction

Record a short video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself and sharing why you want to join the team. Submit a link (Loom or Google Drive).





** Applications submitted without a PDF resume AND a video introduction will not be reviewed. **

title: CRM Assistant
description:
Canine Conversions is a fast-growing digital marketing agency based in the United States. We specialize in running
 and Instagram ads for dog training businesses, helping them generate high-quality leads and scale their companies efficiently.



As our team continues to expand, we’re looking for a skilled and analytical Media Buyer to join our performance marketing department. This is a fantastic opportunity to work with a proven business model, world-class training, and a results-driven team culture. If you’re data-driven, creative, and passionate about delivering measurable results, this role is for you.





Job Summary

We are looking for a detail-oriented CRM Administrator to manage our lead database, optimize workflows, and ensure our sales team (Setters) has the data they need to succeed. You will be the "architect" behind our CRM, ensuring that every lead is tracked, every automation fires correctly, and our reporting is 100?curate.



Day-to-Day Tasks

Database Management: Maintain the integrity of our CRM data by performing regular audits, removing duplicates, and ensuring all lead information is up to date.



Setter Support: Grant and manage access levels for our sales setters, ensuring they have the right tools and training to log their activities.



Workflow Automation: Build and troubleshoot automated
 sequences, SMS triggers, and task reminders within the CRM.



Reporting & Analytics: Generate daily and weekly reports on key performance indicators (KPIs) like lead conversion rates, setter activity, and pipeline health.



Process Optimization: Continuously look for ways to simplify the "Setter" process by reducing manual data entry and improving the lead hand-off flow.



Troubleshooting: Act as the first point of contact for any technical glitches or "confusing information" within the CRM system.



Requirements:



Experience: 2+ years of experience managing CRMs (e.g., GoHighLevel, HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar).

Analytical Mindset: You love spreadsheets and finding patterns in data.

Tech Savvy: Comfortable with Zapier, Slack, and project management tools.

Communication: Ability to explain complex technical processes to non-technical tea
mbers clearly.

title: Data Analyst (SQL-Heavy / Systems-Focused)
description:
DAP Brands is looking for a high-ownership, systems-minded Data Analyst who doesn’t just report numbers; they build and maintain the foundation that powers decision-making.



This role is critical as we move toward a centralized PostgreSQL database and cleaner, more reliable data across all teams.



If you’re someone who sees messy, inconsistent data and thinks “this needs structure, validation, and automation”. Keep reading.



What This Role Really Is



You are not just analyzing data.



You are responsible for:

- Structuring it

- Cleaning and validating it

- Standardizing it across systems

- Making it usable and trustworthy



You ensure that when leadership reviews data, it’s accurate, consistent, and ready for decisions.



Key Responsibilities

- Build, manage, and optimize SQL/PostgreSQL databases for centralized data access

- Write efficient, scalable queries for reporting and operational use

- Design and maintain ETL processes to ensure clean and consistent data flow

- Perform data cleaning, validation, and normalization across multiple data sources

- Ensure data integrity, especially across CRM and operational systems

- Identify inconsistencies, duplicates, and structural issues in datasets

- Create and maintain client-level dashboards and reporting systems

- Develop reports that directly support business and operational decisions

- Collaborate with teams to define metrics and improve data tracking

- Proactively improve how data is collected, stored, and used



Non-Negotiables (Must-Have)

- Strong experience with SQL (PostgreSQL preferred)

- Strong proficiency in data cleaning, validation, and normalization

- Advanced skills in Excel / Google Sheets (pivot tables, queries, data modeling)

- Experience with CRM systems (e.g., GoHighLevel, HubSpot) and maintaining data integrity

- Proficiency in programming/scripting (JavaScript, XML, or similar)

- Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills

close attention to detail

- Proactive mindset and strong ownership



Strong Plus (Will Set You Apart)

- Experience building or managing centralized databases or data warehouses

- Hands-on experience with ETL tools or automation workflows

- Experience with dashboarding tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.)

- Experience handling client-level or multi-entity datasets

- Background in operations, marketing, or financial data analysis

- Experience in fast-paced, high-growth environments



What We Actually Need (Read This Carefully)



Most candidates can analyze data.



We are looking for someone who can:

- Ensure data accuracy across systems (not just within one report)

- Build clean, standardized datasets fro
ssy inputs

- Maintain data integrity inside CRMs and operational tools

- Reduce manual work through automation and better structure

- Take ownership of data issues and fix them without being told



Final Note



We move fast.

We expect ownership.

We value people who fix problems before they’re told.



If you’re looking to “analyze data,” this role is not for you. Apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.



Suppose you want to build the backbone of how a company uses data. We want to talk.

description:
We’re Hiring: Content Creation & Copywriting Virtual Assistant

Remote | Full-Time | US Hours: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM (PST), 5 days/week

We are looking for a proactive, detail-oriented, and fast-learning Content Creation & Copywriting Virtual Assistant to support our real estate sales and marketing team. This role is ideal for a strong writer who understands the U.S. market, can create compelling copy across platforms, and can pair writing with effective visual content in real estate industry.

Responsibilities

- Write and publish high-quality content for LinkedIn, Instagram,
, X 
), and
 marketing.

- Create content calendars and weekly posting plans aligned with marketing campaigns and listings.

- Develop persuasive copy for property marketing, event promotions, buyer/seller education, and lead-generation campaigns.

- Repurpose long-form content (webinars, articles, listings) into short-form social content.

- Design visual content using Canva: posters, flyers, carousels, and social media templates.

- Edit short-form videos for social media (Reels/Shorts), including basic cuts, captions, hooks, and subtitles.

- Research U.S. market trends, competitors, and content formats to propose new content ideas.

- Track content performance and optimize copy based on engagement and results.

- Coordinate with internal teams to ensure timely approvals and execution.

Qualifications

- Minimum 2 years of experience in content writing, copywriting, or social media marketing.

- Strong English writing skills with the ability to adapt tone across platforms.

- Familiarity with U.S. audience behavior and social media trends.

- Proficiency in Canva; experience with video editing tools (CapCut, Canva, Premiere, etc.) preferred.

- Understanding of marketing fundamentals (hooks, storytelling, CTAs).

- Highly organized, deadline-driven, and able to work independently.

- Proficiency in Google Workspace and basic Excel/Sheets.

- Bachelor’s degree (Marketing, Communications, Business, or related).

- Graduates from reputable universities in the Philippines (UP, Ateneo, La Salle, UST, etc.) preferred, though exceptional candidates from other institutions are welcome.

Application Requirement (Important)

To be considered, applicants must submit:

- A portfolio showcasing writing samples and/or social media content (links, PDFs, or screenshots), and/or

- Complete a short writing test provided by our team (social media post or
 copy).

Compensation & Benefits

- Salary: $650 – $1,000/month (based on experience and qualifications).

- Full-time remote role aligned with US PST hours (10:00 AM – 6:00 PM).

- Performance-based bonuses after a 3-month probation.

- Growth opportunities in content strategy, branding, and real estate marketing.

title: IT Device Management Specialist (Samsung Knox)
description:
I'm Hiring!



Type: Fully Remote





Position: IT Device Management Specialist (Remote, PH)



Looking for IT Device Management Specialist to manage and support the mobile device environment used by our clinical teams. This role will primarily focus on managing Android tablets used by nurses and field staff, ensuring devices are reliable, secure, and properly configured for daily operations.



The specialist will own device configuration, enrollment, and ongoing platform improvements while helping scale device management processes as the company grows. This role will work closely with IT leadership, engineering, and operations teams to ensure the device platform continues to support clinical workflows effectively.



Requirements:

- Experience managing **Android devices in an enterprise or large-scale environment**

- Experience with **mobile device management (MDM) platforms**

- Strong troubleshooting experience with mobile device systems

- Experience configuring device policies, profiles, and security settings

- Ability to document processes and work cross-functionally with IT and operations teams

- Experience with **Samsung Knox device management**



To Apply:

Send your updated resume, and a 1–2 minute introduction video

Full job description

Join Our Team as an Office Manager for fast growing company that needs to get Organized to its fullest  

Are you ready to elevate your career in an exhilarating environment? This is a rare and exciting opportunity to become part of one of the most dynamic and rapidly growing HVAC companies! Renowned for our unique culture of excellence and unwavering dedication to outstanding service, we are looking for a passionate Office Manager to lead our operations.

 Growth Opportunities: We're committed to your professional development—if you're eager to learn and grow, this is the place for you!

* Oversee daily office operations, ensuring seamless customer service and efficient dispatch.

* Strong knowledge of order management, permits, and basic accounting principles.

* Excellent leadership skills with a track record of managing customer service teams.

* Ability to multitask and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

* Outstanding communication skills and keen attention to detail.

* Proficiency in office management software (QuickBooks, Housecall Pro  goggle etc.).

* Is Fluent in spanish 

Ready to Make an Impact??If you’re looking for an incredible opportunity to grow your career and be part of something truly special, don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime chance! Apply now and take the first step toward a rewarding career with us!

Job Types: Full-time, Part-time

Pay: $4.00per hour up to 8.00per hour 

Expected hours: 40 per week plus some weekends

title: Real Estate Cold Caller (Lead Generation Specialist)
description:
We are looking for a reliable and experienced Cold Caller & Lead Manager to join our real estate investment company. Our business helps homeowners sell their homes quickly by providing fast cash offers. PLEASE PROVIDE A SAMPLE CALL WHEN REACHING OUT TO US. 



Your role will include cold calling homeowners, answering inbound calls, responding to text messages, and managing leads inside Go High Level (GHL). You will be the first point of contact with potential sellers and will help identify motivated homeowners.



This is a long-term position for someone who is professional, organized, and comfortable communicating with homeowners.



Responsibilities



• Cold call homeowners from provided lead lists

• Answer inbound calls from potential sellers

• Respond to incoming text messages from leads

• Qualify homeowners and identify motivated sellers

• Follow our cold calling and texting scripts

• Schedule appointments for the acquisitions team

• Update notes, tags, and lead status in Go High Level (GHL)

• Maintain organized communication with all leads



Requirements



• Experience with cold calling required

• Experience using Go High Level (GHL) preferred

• Strong English speaking and writing skills

• Comfortable answering inbound calls and texts

• Friendly and confident phone presence

• Ability to handle objections and rejection

• Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

• Ability to make 100+ calls per day



Compensation



• Competitive hourly pay based on experience

• Performance bonuses for qualified leads and appointments

• Long-term opportunity with room for growth

title: Operations Virtual Assistant
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Operations Virtual Assistant

(Process-Driven | High-Precision | Behind-the-Scenes Operations)



Job Overview



Maid In America Cleaning Services is hiring an Operations Virtual Assistant to protect schedule integrity, data accuracy, and operational consistency.



This is a behind-the-scenes execution role. You will not be customer-facing. You will work inside defined systems and SOPs to ensure our operational engine runs cleanly and efficiently every day.



If you enjoy structured work, moving quickly without sacrificing accuracy, and taking pride in clean execution, this role will feel natural to you.



What You’ll Do:



1. Scheduling & Job Management (Primary Responsibility)

You will own scheduling preparation and system accuracy for our TN locations.



This includes:

- Creating and modifying clients, jobs, and visits in Jobber

- Preparing future-day schedules for review and approval

- Creating training tasks in Jobber for new tea
mbers

- Re-sequencing jobs and rebalancing teams within defined guardrails

- Maintaining week-to-week schedule consistency whenever possible

- Flagging and correcting conflicts or inefficiencies early

- Executing same-day scheduling adjustments exactly as directed



2. Data Accuracy & System Integrity

You will ensure operational data is accurate, complete, and reliable.



This includes:

- Updating customer notes, job frequencies, and service details

- Invoicing customers promptly after service completion

- Calculating cost-of-service data after jobs

- Preparing timesheet and tips reports for payroll

- Logging complaints and rectified cleans accurately

- Creating and publishing weekly operational reports

- Manual KPI data entry

- Verifying work-to-labor ratios using company tools



3. Administrative Support

You will support operational structure and remove administrative friction.



This includes:

- Supporting the Assistant Operations Manager with administrative execution

- Maintaining internal trackers and shared documents

- Following established documentation and checklists consistently



What This Role Requires

- High pace with high accuracy

- Comfort with structured, repeatable tasks

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication when issues arise

- Strong adherence defined systems and guardrails

- Ability to work independently within clear expectations



This role involves a high volume of operational tasks that must be completed correctly and consistently. As the company grows, volume will increase — and this role grows with it.



Who We’re Looking For



Detail-Oriented & Reliable

You take pride in doing things correctly and thoroughly.



High Throughput Executor

You can handle volume without losing structure or focus.



Process-Driven

You work well inside defined systems and appreciate clear expectations.



Accountable Communicator

You flag issues early, own mistakes quickly, and ask for clarification when needed.



Tech-Comfortable

You’re comfortable using tools like Slack, Google Suite, Chrome extensions, and scheduling platforms. Experience with Jobber is a strong plus.



Experienced in Fast-Paced Admin or Scheduling

Background in administrative, dispatch, logistics, scheduling, or operations-heavy roles is

preferred.



Work Requirements

- Monday–Friday, 8AM–5PM U.S. Central Time

- Reliable internet with backup

- Dedicated workspace

- Backup power options in case of outages

- Fluent written English



Our Core Values

- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If these values resonate with you, we want to hear from you.



Important Screening Step (Read Carefully)



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

1. Complete our Culture Index survey: 

2. Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value you most resonate with (ALL CAPS).

3. Use a minimum of 5 Apply Points when applying

4. In your application message, include one sentence describing the most repetitive task you have performed at a high standard for 6+ months.



Applications that do not follow all instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Amazon Fba Wholesale/OA VA
description:
We are launching a brand new Amazon FBA business and need an experienced Manager with a proven track record in both Wholesale and Online Arbitrage (OA) to help us build from the ground up. This is a full A-Z management role. We provide the tools; you provide the expertise to get our new account profitable.



Note: You will be provided with access to our software extensions and Accounts. (Keepa, SmartScout, AsinZen, etc.) to streamline your workflow.

Key Responsibilities:



* New Account Growth: Strategically sourcing to build initial sales velocity and account health.

* Dual-Model Sourcing: Identify profitable leads using Keepa, SmartScout, and AsinZen.

* Profit Standards: All leads must meet a minimum profit of $3 per unit or a 20% ROI.

* Wholesale Outreach: Contact brands and distributors to open professional accounts from scratch.

* Ungating: Lead the process for getting our new store ungated in restricted brands and categories.

* Logistics & Prep: Coordinate with wholesalers and our Prep Center to ensure products are delivered to Amazon FBA correctly and on time.

* A-Z Account Management: Complete oversight of Seller Central.



Requirements:



* Proven Experience: A solid, demonstrable history of success in both Amazon Wholesale and OA.

* Tool Mastery: High proficiency in Keepa, SmartScout, and AsinZen.

* Communication: Professional English skills for supplier outreach.

* Reliability: Ability to work independently. Work whenever you like, as long as the work is done and delivered on a timely manner.



Perks:



* Ultimate Flexibility: Full control over your schedule.

* Flexible Time Off: Available upon request.

* Bi-weekly Payments: Reliable pay every two weeks.



How to Apply:



   1. Briefly summarize your proven track record in Wholesale and OA.

   2. What is your favorite category for sourcing and why?

   3. Video Submission: Provide a link (Loom, Google Drive, etc.) to a short video telling us why you are a good fit for a brand-new account launch. Applications without a video will not be considered.

title: Web Developer
description:

Performance optimization

Soft Skills

Excellent attention to detail

Good communication skills

Time management and ability to meet deadlines

Work Setup:

-Work hours: 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM Pacific Standard Time (with 30-minute lunch break)

title: Real Estate Buyer-Lead Ads Specialist & Short-Term Rental Marketing Manager
description:

We want someone who understands real estate marketing, local targeting, hospitality-style creatives, and conversion systems.



Your job is simple:

Bring us buyers and drive bookings.

Company: Kobla Homes / Kobla Group

Residential Listings: 19 properties in Texarkana, 4 in Houston

Short-Term Rentals: 4 properties in Texas

title: ???? STR Marketing Expert (Direct Bookings + Corporate Outreach + Paid Ads) – Luxury Properties
description:
Summary

ABOUT US



Thompson Luxury Properties operates a portfolio of 4 luxury short-term rental homes in Houston (911–917 Thompson St, 77007). We cater to high-end travelers, corporate groups, and extended stays. Our homes are fully furnished, premium, and located in one of Houston’s most desirable areas.



Currently, our portfolio averages ~70% occupancy and $600–$700/night rates. Our target is 85%+ occupancy and $850+/night across all properties. We are seeking a high-performance STR Marketing Expert who knows how to execute fast and can help us hit these metrics in the next 60–90 days.



ROLE OVERVIEW



We are looking for a hands-on marketing leader (not just a strategist) who can plan, build, and execute across multiple channels to generate direct bookings, corporate contracts, and sustainable revenue growth.



This is a performance-focused role where success will be measured by occupancy growth, direct booking volume, and average nightly rate achieved.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



You will be responsible for creating and executing a full-stack marketing system for Thompson Properties, including:



? Website & Conversion Funnel

? Build an improved, modern direct booking website optimized for conversions.

? Integrate with our CRM & booking engine.

? Implement booking funnels, landing pages, and retargeting systems.

? CRM &
 Marketing

? Build a data-driven
 marketing system for repeat guests, corporate clients, and leads.

? Automate personalized booking reminders, upsells, and loyalty campaigns.

? Drive at least 20–30% of monthly bookings via
 within 60–90 days.

? Corporate Outreach & Partnerships

? Execute outreach campaigns to corporate housing managers, HR departments, relocation firms, and travel coordinators.

? Build partnerships with local hospitals, oil & gas companies, law firms, and universities to secure consistent bookings.

? Develop scripts + outreach sequences 
 + LinkedIn + phone).

? Paid Ads & Performance Marketing

? Run profitable ad campaigns (Meta, Google, TikTok) targeting direct bookings.

? Implement retargeting ads for website visitors and past guests.

? Scale campaigns with clear ROI, targeting $5–$10 per lead and positive ROAS.

? Organic Marketing & SEO

? Optimize listings for ranking (Airbnb, VRBO, Google Travel).

? Implement SEO for the booking site to rank for “Houston luxury rentals,” “corporate rentals in Houston,” etc.

? Build organic traffic via Instagram + TikTok strategy showcasing the homes and neighborhood.

? Revenue Growth Execution

? Aggressively execute on all strategies to move the portfolio from ~70% to 85%+ occupancy and $600–700/night to $850+/night within 90 days.

? Work with dynamic pricing tools (PriceLabs, Beyond) and pair them with marketing to maximize RevPAR.



REQUIREMENTS

???? Proven success in short-term rental (STR), vacation rental, or hospitality marketing.

???? Strong portfolio of direct booking campaigns, corporate outreach wins, and revenue growth results.

Hands-on expertise with:

???? Website + funnel building (WordPress, Squarespace, Lodgify, or similar).

???? CRM +
 automation (HubSpot, ActiveCampaign, or similar).

???? Paid ads (Google, Meta, TikTok).

???? SEO + content strategy for STRs.

???? Ability to set strategy AND execute (no pure consultants).

???? Clear understanding of Houston’s STR and corporate rental market is a plus.



What Success Looks Like (60–90 Days)



???? Improved direct booking website live and converting.

???? Automated
 marketing driving at least 20% of new bookings.

???? Corporate outreach delivering 2–3 signed housing contracts.

???? Paid ads generating positive ROI with scalable campaigns.

???? Occupancy consistently hitting 85%+.

???? Average nightly rate increasing to $850+.



COMPENSATION



Budget: $1,500/month retainer (performance bonuses available for hitting KPIs early).



Flexible contract (remote OK).



Long-term growth opportunity if results are achieved.



How to Apply



Send us:



1. A short intro explaining your experience with STRs or hospitality marketing.



2. 2–3 examples of campaigns you’ve run (direct bookings, corporate outreach, paid ads).



3. A 30–60 day outline of how you’d hit 85% occupancy and $850/night for our Houston properties.

title: Senior Video Editor & Motion Graphics Specialist (Remote, Part-Time, UK Hours)
description:
ABOUT DESIGN MAKERS CO



Design Makers Co creates high-impact social content for brands in events/AV & production, housing/property, and dental. We specialize in viral-ready Reels, TikToks, Shorts, and high-performing carousels.



ROLE SNAPSHOT



We are hiring a high-level Video Editor skilled in cinematic editing and clean social design.



This is a remote position, 20 hours per week.

You must be available from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM UK Time.



WHAT YOU’LL DO



- Edit cinematic short-form videos for Reels, TikTok, and Shorts.

- Create motion graphics within your edits, including animated hooks, kinetic typography, tracking, masking, and smooth transitions.

- Apply strong colour grading and sound design.

- Design branded carousels and static posts.

- Deliver platform-ready formats (9:16, 1:1, 16:9).

- Maintain organized project files.



MUST-HAVE SKILLS



- Advanced skills in Premiere Pro and After Effects.

- Strong cinematic storytelling and pacing.

- Proven motion graphics ability (not template-only editing).

- Solid Photoshop and Illustrator skills.

- Recent portfolio in events, housing/property, or dental.

- Strong English communication and fast turnaround time.



APPLICATION PROCESS



Step 1: Submit your portfolio link for initial review.

Once we have reviewed your portfolio, we will message you if you can proceed to the next step.



Step 2: If selected, you must send the following:

- 3 to 5 short-form videos that show advanced word animation.

- Two examples of scroll-stopping short-form edits that demonstrate advanced editing.

- Two examples where you have used AI tools in your workflow.

- A short explanation of your editing process.

- A list of the software you use.

- Confirmation that you can work 20 hours per week at $8 per hour.

- Your average turnaround time per short-form video.



HIRING PROCESS



- Portfolio review.

- Advanced submission review.

- Trial task (cinematic Reel and graphic carousel).

- Short recruiter interview.

- Final technical walkthrough with the Company Director.

title: Appointment Setter
description:
We’re a fast-growing Business Funding Company helping entrepreneurs access up to $100,000+ in funding. We’re looking for a hungry, consistent, and reliable Appointment Setter who knows how to turn conversations into booked calls.



If you can confidently start conversations, build trust fast, and move people to take action… keep reading.



What You’ll Be Doing



Message 100+ new leads daily (Instagram,
, TikTok, etc.)

Respond to inbound messages and qualify prospects

Start conversations that lead to booked calls on our calendar

Follow up with cold, warm, and hot leads consistently

Track conversations and appointments in CRM

Hit daily and weekly booking targets



Who We’re Looking For



Experience in appointment setting, sales, or DM outreach

Strong communication skills (you don’t sound like a robot)

Comfortable handling objections and keeping conversations flowing

Consistent, disciplined, and actually follows instructions

Familiar with credit repair, business funding, or finance (preferred, not required)

Available full-time and ready to work



Compensation

Send a message with:



Your experience with appointment setting or sales

Your availability (hours per day/week)



Why you’re a good fit for this role



People who just “need a job” won’t last here.

We’re looking for someone who wants to win, earn, and grow.



If that’s you, apply now.

description:
