title: Full-Time SaaS Customer Success Manager (UK Hours) - Paid in GBP
description:
Are you a hungry, driven Customer Success professional who actually wants to build something massive?



We are OnCare, a fast-growing UK-based SaaS company. We build a care management software for home care and companionship agencies. We currently have over 500 agencies using our platform, and we are aggressively expanding into the US market.



Right now, I am looking for ONE highly motivated Customer Success Manager to join our team full-time.



I am not looking for a standard "ticket-answerer." I am looking for someone who has serious drive, loves the SaaS world, and wants to eventually become our Head of Customer Success and build out their own local team.



The Schedule:



This is a full-time, dedicated role. You must be online and highly responsive during UK business hours: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM (UK Time), Monday through Friday.



What You Will Actually Be Doing:



Once the sales team closes a deal, the client is yours. You own the relationship.



The Welcome & Onboarding: You will run live Zoom setup calls with new agency owners. You need to make them feel welcome, comfortable, and confident using our software.



Retention & Upselling: You will track their usage and make sure they never want to cancel. If you see an opportunity where they could benefit from an extra feature or upgrade, you confidently upsell them.



Problem Solving: You will handle questions and potential bugs. Agency owners are stressed and busy—you need to have endless patience, a big smile, and calm energy.



The Product Loop: You are the voice of the customer. You will work directly with our tech team to report issues and explain what we need to develop next to keep our clients thrilled.



Who You Are:



Perfect English (Mandatory): You must have an excellent level of spoken English with a very clear US or UK accent. You will be on video with business owners every single day.



You have "The Hunger": You are highly motivated, you love coming to work, and you want to absolutely crush your goals.



Perfectionist & Tech-Savvy: You are obsessed with the details, you learn new software instantly, and you are extremely proactive in your communication with our internal team.



The Perks & The Future:



Paid in GBP (£): Highly competitive, reliable monthly compensation.



Limitless Evolution: If you come in and crush this role, you will evolve into the Head of Customer Success. You will get the budget to hire and manage your own team on the ground.



HOW TO APPLY (Read Carefully):



I get a lot of automated spam, so to prove you actually read this and have high attention to detail, you MUST do two things:



Start your cover letter with the exact phrase: "ONCARE PH-CSM"



Include a link to a 2min video recording introducing yourself and why you are the best person for this position.



If you don't include the exact phrase AND a personnalized video recording for OnCare, your application will be instantly declined. I can't wait to meet you!

title: Sales Executive
description:
About Us



CR Movers Inc. is a fast-growing professional moving company based in Toronto, Canada. We handle residential and commercial moves across the Greater Toronto Area and all over Canada. As demand continues to grow, we are expanding our sales team.



We are looking for a results-driven Sales Executive who can confidently convert inbound and outbound leads into booked moves.



Role Overview



This is not a passive customer service role. We are looking for someone with 5 to 10 years of strong sales experience and a proven track record of closing deals and exceeding targets.



You will be responsible for closing deals, handling objections, and turning leads into confirmed bookings. We are looking for someone who does not accept “no” without a valid reason, understands urgency, and knows how to guide clients toward a decision.



Key Responsibilities



Contact inbound leads via phone, SMS, WhatsApp, and

Qualify leads and close bookings for moving services

Handle objections confidently (price, timing, competition, hesitation)

Follow up persistently until a clear yes or no is received

Book confirmed jobs directly into our system

Maintain accurate notes and updates in CRM

Meet or exceed weekly booking targets

Respond to new leads within minutes to maximize conversion

Communicate professionally with Canadian clients



What We’re Looking For



? Proven sales closing experience (service-based industry preferred)

? Strong English communication (spoken & written)

? Confident, persuasive, and assertive personality

? Ability to handle objections without sounding aggressive

? Highly organized with strong follow-up discipline

? Comfortable working with scripts but able to adapt naturally

? Strong internet connection and quiet work environment

? Willingness to work Eastern Standard Time (Toronto-based)

? Open to working on weekends



Sales Mindset Required



You ask for the sale

You don’t ghost leads

You don’t accept “maybe”

You understand urgency and booking psychology

You can explain value, not just price

You follow up until there is a clear outcome



Tools You’ll Use



CRM system (training provided)

VoIP / Calling software



Why Join CR Movers?



Stable monthly pay

Performance incentives for strong closers

Clear expectations and structure

High volume of inbound leads

Long-term opportunity with a growing company



How to Apply



Please
 your resume and cover letter to 



Please include:



A brief summary of your sales closing experience

Examples of objections you’ve handled and how you closed

Your availability and internet speed (weekend availability preferred, EST schedule)

title: Sales Manager
description:
About Us



CR Movers Inc. is a fast-growing professional moving company based in Toronto, Canada. We handle residential and commercial moves across the Greater Toronto Area and across Canada. As demand continues to grow, we are expanding our sales leadership team.



We are looking for a results-driven Sales Manager who can lead from the front—closing deals, managing a team, and driving revenue growth.



Role Overview



This is a hands-on leadership role, not just management. We are looking for someone with a minimum of 8 to 10 years of sales experience, including leadership, with a proven track record of closing deals and leading high-performing teams.



You will be responsible for owning the sales pipeline, coaching closers, and ensuring leads are converted into booked moves. You must be someone who does not accept “no” without a valid reason and knows how to drive urgency across a team.



Key Responsibilities



Manage and lead a team of sales representatives (local and/or overseas)

Set daily, weekly, and monthly booking targets and ensure they are met

Monitor lead flow and ensure rapid response times (calls within minutes)

Actively close deals (high-value or complex bookings)

Train, coach, and develop sales reps on objection handling and closing techniques

Review calls and provide feedback to improve conversion rates

Ensure consistent follow-ups until a clear yes or no is received

Oversee CRM usage, pipeline tracking, and reporting accuracy

Identify gaps in the sales process and implement improvements

Communicate professionally with Canadian clients and handle escalations when needed



What We’re Looking For



? Proven sales leadership experience (service-based industry preferred)

? Strong closing ability—you can both sell and teach others to sell

? Excellent English communication (spoken & written)

? Confident, persuasive, and assertive leadership style

? Strong organizational and follow-up discipline

? Experience managing remote or overseas teams is a strong asset

? Comfortable working with scripts, KPIs, and performance tracking

? Strong internet connection and professional work environment

? Willingness to work on Eastern Standard Time (Toronto-based)

? Weekend availability required



Sales Leadership Mindset Required



You lead by example and close deals yourself

You hold your team accountable to performance

You don’t tolerate missed follow-ups or slow responses

You understand urgency, pipeline management, and booking psychology

You coach your team to sell value, not just compete on price

You turn “maybes” into decisions



Tools You’ll Use



CRM system (training provided)

VoIP / Calling software

Reporting dashboards & sales trackers



Why Join CR Movers?



Stable monthly pay + performance incentives

Leadership role with direct impact on company growth

Opportunity to build and scale a high-performing sales team

Clear structure, KPIs, and expectations

Long-term career growth with a fast-scaling company



How to Apply



Please
 your resume and cover letter to 



Include:



A brief summary of your sales leadership and closing experience

Examples of how you’ve handled objections and coached others to close

Your availability (including weekends) and internet speed

title: Lead Credit Repair Specialist
description:
About Us: We are a trusted leader in credit repair, committed to helping individuals take control of their financial future. Our mission is to empower our clients with proven strategies to improve their credit standing and achieve financial stability. 

We're looking for a driven and detail-oriented Credit Repair Specialist to join our growing team and make a real impact.



Key Responsibilities:

• Analyze client credit reports to identify inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable information.

• Initiate and follow up on disputes by directly contacting credit reporting agencies (such as Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion) via phone, mail, or online portals on behalf of clients.

• Develop and implement personalized credit repair strategies tailored to each client's unique financial situation.

• Communicate with clients to gather supporting documentation and explain the dispute process clearly.

• Provide consistent updates on dispute progress and credit changes.

• Maintain accurate, detailed records of all correspondence, disputes, and client interactions.

• Stay informed about credit industry laws, including the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), and apply them effectively in client cases.

• Deliver empathetic and professional customer service throughout the credit repair journey.



Qualifications:

• 5+ years of experience in credit repair, credit counseling, or a related role.

• Strong understanding of credit reports, scoring models, and dispute procedures.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

• Highly organized, self-motivated, and capable of managing multiple cases simultaneously.

• Tech-savvy with proficiency in credit repair platforms and Microsoft Office tools.

• Familiarity with consumer protection laws (FCRA, FDCPA) is a strong plus.



Why You'll Love Working With Us:

• Positive and collaborative work culture

• Remote flexibility (if applicable)

• Ongoing professional development and training

• Opportunity to make a meaningful difference in people's lives



Additional Role Expectations & Requirements:

We are looking for someone who fully understands how a credit repair company should operate. Ideal candidates must:

• Be proficient in using tools such as CRC, Dispute Fox, FTC Disputes, and CFPB 



Complaint Portals

• Know how to effectively leverage the language of U.S. FCRA laws to clean up consumer reports

• Be experienced in removing outdated personal information (e.g., old addresses) and calling consumer reporting agencies (TransUnion, Experian, Equifax) directly

• Have a strong background in consumer rights, credit reporting, and dispute documentation

• Be comfortable speaking English fluently and clearly over the phone - perfect spoken English is required

• Possess strong customer service and sales skills, including onboarding clients and managing communications via phone, text, or

• Be reliable, have fast internet, and be able to perform mailing, faxing, and online disputes

title: Marketing Specialist & Systems Architect
description:
Executive Summary



We are seeking a rare hybrid talent: a Marketing Specialist & Systems Architect who possesses the strategic vision of a brand manager and the technical execution skills of a digital automator. You will not only be responsible for launching and managing multi-channel campaigns (digital and print) but will also be tasked with building the "machine" behind the marketing—optimizing websites for SEO, managing CRM workflows in GoHighLevel, and ensuring that automation is the answer to every scaling challenge.

Key Responsibilities



Strategic Implementation:

Design and execute comprehensive social media and print campaigns that align with brand identity and ROI goals. You will bridge the gap between high-level strategy and daily execution.



Full-Stack Web & SEO:

Take complete ownership of website management, ensuring all digital properties are high-performing, secure, and aggressively optimized for search engines to drive organic growth.



Automation & Systems:

Implement and manage complex workflows within GoHighLevel and other scheduling platforms. You will be expected to find efficiencies in every process to reduce manual overhead.



AI Integration:

Leverage AI as a force multiplier for content ideation, data analysis, and efficiency without sacrificing the "human touch" or brand authenticity.



Multi-Channel Marketing:

Seamlessly pivot between digital ad spend, social media engagement, and traditional print marketing materials to create a cohesive brand experience.

Technical & Professional Requirements

Skill Category Requirement

Marketing Mastery 3-5 years in Digital & Print marketing with a portfolio of successful social media campaigns.

Systems & CRM Professional proficiency in GoHighLevel (funnels, workflows, snapshots) and content schedulers.

Web & SEO Proven ability to fully manage CMS platforms (WordPress/Wix/GHL) and execute technical SEO audits.

Automation Mindset Demonstrated experience building automated lead-gen or nurture sequences.

The "Edge" (Coding) Basic to intermediate knowledge of HTML, CSS, or JavaScript to customize web elements and integrations.

AI Literacy Ability to use AI for efficiency while maintaining high-quality, non-generic output.

Why This Role is Unique



We aren't looking for a "virtual assistant" to simply check off tasks. We are looking for a specialist who understands that automation is the key to scale. You will have the autonomy to build and refine our marketing tech stack. You will be the engine that allows our brands to show up everywhere, efficiently, while maintaining the tactical flexibility of a boutique operation.

Candidate Profile



    The Growth Architect: You don't just "post" to social media; you build systems that convert followers into leads.



    The Technical Creative: You are as comfortable looking at a line of code or an SEO dashboard as you are designing a brand-aligned print flyer.



    The AI-Augmented Pro: You use AI to work 10x faster, but you have the discernment to know when a human eye is non-negotiable.



Work Environment



This is a fully virtual position offering flexibility to work from anywhere while maintaining collaborative relationships with tea
mbers and clients across various time zones. This is initially a contract role with a strong path toward permanent placement for a high-performer who proves their value through systems and results.

Application Process



To be considered for this position, please visit: www[.]goodelife[.]net/careers



    Note: Due to our strict HR Policy, applications will not be considered if they are not received through the official careers site linked above.



This position requires candidates to successfully complete our comprehensive evaluation process, which includes technical assessments and multiple interview stages to ensure the best fit for both the role and our organizational culture.

title: Structural Engineer
description:
Location: Work from Home



Type of Employment: Full-time



Work Shift: USA Business Hours



Position Overview:



We are seeking a highly skilled Structural Engineer fluent in Metal Structures and Wood Frame Structures for Residential and Commercial Real Estate projects. The ideal candidate will have substantial experience in structural engineering, particularly in designing steel and wood frame structures for the real estate industry.



Responsibilities:



Design a new 14 ft x 24 ft steel frame:

Carry a 15K - 20K lbs modular housing structure from factory to foundation on a flat-bed truck.

Ensure the frame withstands the stress of 2-point loading (spanning 24ft) within 1-inch deflection at mid-point during the lift procedure from 6 inches above floor to truck bed height of 32 inches above grade via a tilt-bed truck.

Ensure the frame withstands loading at the factory to unloading to foundation in the field (32 inches above grade to 20 inches above grade).

Design a steel frame structure that is lightweight and easy to fabricate, requiring less than 30 man-hours (of cutting, drilling, welding, etc.).

F40 - Strengthen existing 12 ft x 40 ft steel chassis design:

Re-engineer existing steel frame chassis structures to reduce deflection at midpoint during the lift procedure.

Address current deflection issues that cause damage to the finished interiors of the residential unit (drywall cracking, buckling).

14x24 Foundation - with PE Seal:

Design and seal (if possible) reinforced concrete strip pier foundations for 14 ft x 24 ft modular housing units.

Utilize examples of existing 12 ft x 40 ft foundations as references.

14x24 First & Second Story:

Provide structural/architectural construction details for the first floor (module 1) to support the second floor (module 2).

Ensure modules are constructed in the modular building factory, shipped to the site, and the second floor is lifted by crane to sit upon the first floor module.

R32 Gable Roof Design:

Develop structural design details for a gable roof with a peak at midpoint (longitudinally) that supports all live and static loads when mounted on the foundation and withstands transitory stresses during the loading/unloading process and shipping across highways in Texas.

R32 Pop-up Roof Structural:

Create modular roof and side wall elements that allow transporting modular units with the roof element laying flat with a maximum package height of 12 ft (grade to highest point) and be able to lift the roof elements into place on a pivot to full height (height to be designed) in the field.

Ensure finish construction fixes the structure into place, weatherproofs and seals, and completes the finish-out of the final unit (unit will be strapped to the foundation - not to be moved again).

Top Deck Weatherproofing System:

Create the proper structural and architectural details required for a rooftop deck above the modular housing that is weather-tight and low maintenance.

Ideal Candidate Requirements:



Primary Experience:



Structural Engineer:

Professional Engineer

Has direct work experience in Structural Engineering for a USA-based Client.

Experience in completing structural designs for both steel and wood frame structures in the real estate industry.

Access to necessary tools to perform load calculations for all types of materials.

Ability to translate designs into layman's terms, detailed specifications, and resulting plans for metal fabrication.

Direct work experience with USA firms preparing design submittals for construction preferably in Texas.

Secondary Experience:



Architectural Engineering:

Familiarity with USA residential and commercial housing projects.

Ability to read, revise, and publish architectural plans.

Has experience working as an architect or for a USA-based architect.

Access to necessary tools to read and create construction drawings in CAD, AutoCAD, Revit, etc.

Benefits:



Permanent work from home

Ongoing training and professional development opportunities

Collaborative and supportive work environment

Competitive salary

title: High-Performance Executive Operator (Data & Operations)
description:
This is not a traditional Executive Assistant role.



We are looking for a high-performance operator who can combine data intelligence with operational execution and work directly alongside a fast-moving, visionary leader.



This role is for someone who can bring structure to data, clarity to decisions, and execution to leadership initiatives.



If you thrive in fast environments, think analytically, and operate with ownership, keep reading.



Who We Are:



DAP Brands is a community of high performers building businesses that create real impact.



Our leadership team moves quickly, thinks strategically, and expects people to take responsibility rather than wait for instructions.



We value:

• Speed

• Accountability

• Emotional maturity

• Precision

• Ownership

• Loyalty to the mission (not ego)



What This Role Really Is:



This role combines Data Analysis + Executive Operations.



Your first responsibility is to bring order to data across the organization: Cleaning, structuring, and translating it into clear insights.



Once the data foundation is organized, you will operate as a high-level executive, helping leadership execute faster and smarter.



You will help:



• Clean, organize, and analyze business data

• Turn raw data into meaningful reports and insights

• Support leadership with decision-ready information

• Protect your leader’s time and priorities

• Enforce accountability across leadership initiatives

• Translate strategy into structured execution

• Identify problems before they become fires

• Move initiatives forward without constant direction

• Bring clarity to complex situations



You will sit close to leadership.

You will see how decisions are made.

You must handle that with professionalism, discretion, and maturity.



What This Role Is NOT



This role is not for someone who:



• Needs constant praise or reassurance

• Struggles with direct feedback

• Prefers slow corporate environments

• Gets overwhelmed by changing priorities

• Waits for instructions instead of solving problems

• Is uncomfortable working with data and systems

• Frequently job hops every year



We operate in a high-accountability environment.



If you need tone cushioning or excessive structure, this will likely feel uncomfortable.



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days):



Within your first 90 days, you:



• Organize and clean key business data sets

• Create structured reporting for leadership decisions

• Build dashboards or reports that bring visibility to operations

• Run leadership meetings with clear structure and follow-through

• Anticipate your leader’s needs before they are spoken

• Track priorities and ensure accountability across teams

• Bring solutions instead of just raising issues

• Operate calmly and clearly under pressure



The Profile That Thrives Here



You likely:



• Have experience in data analysis, reporting, or business analytics

• Are comfortable working with spreadsheets, dashboards, and structured data

• Have supported a founder or senior executive before

• Have stayed 3+ years in at least one meaningful role

• Enjoy solving complex operational problems

• Can build systems and workflows from scratch

• Communicate clearly and directly

• Are emotionally regulated and professional under pressure

• Think in outcomes and insights, not just tasks



You are someone who:



• Notices patterns others miss

• Brings clarity where others see chaos

• Moves things forward without being asked twice



Compensation & Growth:



This role offers:



• Competitive compensation aligned with performance

• Direct exposure to executive decision-making

• The opportunity to shape internal systems and operations

• Long-term growth as the company scales



At DAP Brands, we reward impact and ownership, not just tenure.



Final Note



This role is for someone who wants to build, solve, and operate at a high level.



If you enjoy working with data, solving operational problems, and supporting leadership execution, this could be an excellent fit.



If you're looking for a comfortable, slow-paced corporate role, this probably isn't it.



But if you want to operate at a high level and help build something meaningful, apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.

mbers to keep workflows organized and on track

title: Paid Ads Specialist
description:
About the Company

Keystone Click is a Milwaukee-based B2B digital marketing agency that helps manufacturers, professional services firms, and growth-stage companies build pipelines through strategy-led digital marketing. We punch above our weight, combining sharp strategy with hands-on execution across SEO, content, paid media, and marketing automation. Our team is collaborative, process-driven, and committed to measurable results.



Position Overview

We’re looking for a Part-Time Paid Social Media Specialist based in the Philippines to own the day-to-day planning, execution, and optimization of paid social campaigns across 
 and
/Meta for our portfolio of B2B clients. You’ll work closely with our Account Manager, content team, and account strategists to translate client goals into high-performing ad campaigns that generate qualified leads and measurable pipelines. This is a part-time contract role at a rate of $10/hr, with a strong possibility to grow into a full-time position based on performance and business needs.



Key Responsibilities

- Plan, build, launch, and manage paid social campaigns on LinkedIn Campaign Manager and Meta Ads Manager 
 & Instagram) across multiple client accounts.

- Develop audience targeting strategies including custom audiences, lookalike audiences, retargeting segments, and ABM-style targeting on LinkedIn.

- Write compelling ad copy and collaborate with creative resources on ad visuals, carousels, and video assets tailored to B2B audiences.

- Monitor campaign performance daily; optimize for KPIs such as CPL, CTR, ROAS, and conversion rates through A/B testing, bid adjustments, and budget reallocation.

- Build and maintain campaign dashboards and performance reports for internal stakeholders and client-facing delivery.

- Conduct competitive research and audience insights analysis to inform campaign strategy and messaging.

- Manage ad spend budgets responsibly, ensuring pacing aligns with monthly and quarterly targets.

- Stay current with platform algorithm changes, new ad formats, and paid social best practices – proactively bringing recommendations to the team.

- Coordinate with the content and SEO teams to align paid efforts with organic strategy and ensure consistent messaging.



Qualified Applicants

- 3+ years of hands-on experience managing paid social campaigns on 
 and
/Meta for B2B clients or agencies.

- Demonstrated expertise in LinkedIn Campaign Manager (Sponsored Content, Message Ads, Lead Gen Forms, Conversation Ads) and Meta Ads Manager.

- Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable optimization decisions.

- Experience building audience segments, retargeting funnels, and multi-touch attribution models.

- Proficiency with analytics and reporting tools (Google Analytics, Looker Studio, or equivalent).

- Excellent written English communication skills – you’ll write ad copy and collaborate across time zones via Slack,
, and project management tools.

- Self-starter mentality with strong time management; comfortable working independently in a remote, async-first environment.

- Portfolio or case studies showing measurable paid social results (lead volume, CPL reduction, ROAS improvement, etc.).



Preferred Qualifications

- Experience working with U.S.-based B2B agencies or clients in manufacturing, or professional services.

- Basic graphic design skills (Canva, Figma, or Adobe Creative Suite) for quick ad creative iterations.

- Understanding of SEO and content marketing principles and how paid and organic strategies complement each other.



How To Apply

Send your resume, a brief cover letter, and 1–2 portfolio examples of B2B paid social campaigns you’ve managed. Please include metrics that demonstrate your impact (leads generated, cost-per-lead, ROAS, etc.). We’d love to see what you can do.

title: AI Creative Specialist
description:
About the role:

This is not a traditional creative role. You won't be waiting on briefs or polishing decks. You'll be on the frontier testing new AI tools before anyone else, turning them into a real creative advantage, and raising the bar for what our brand looks and feels like.



You'll own AI-driven creative production end-to-end: product imagery, motion, localization, and the strategic thinking behind why we test what we test. If you're the person who finds a new tool and immediately thinks "here's how we could use this", this is your job.



What you'll do: 

- Own AI tool testing and research

- Continuously evaluate emerging AI tools, build structured POVs on what to test and why, and report findings to the team with clear creative use cases.

- Create realistic AI product shots

- Use AI image tools to produce studio-quality product photography at speed — without a studio. Output should be indistinguishable from a high-end shoot.

- Turn static ads into short-form GIFs

- Animate existing static creative into 5–8 second GIFs using AI motion tools — extending asset life and expanding our ad format mix without extra shoot days.

- Translate ads into other languages

- Use AI translation and design tools to adapt static ads for new markets — maintaining visual integrity and brand voice across languages.

- Stay ahead of the curve. Bring ideas to the table. Tell us what tools are worth our time, what's overhyped, and what could actually change how we work.



You're the right fit if:

- You already use AI tools daily and have a strong opinion on which ones are actually good

- You have a creative eye. You know what "good" looks like and you won't settle for mediocre output

- You move fast. You'd rather ship a test today than perfect it next week

- You're obsessed with DTC, CPG, or performance marketing creative — you know what converts

- You communicate clearly. Your reports and recommendations are sharp, not vague

- You're comfortable working async and remotely — you don't need hand-holding to figure out what to work on



How to apply:

Send us your portfolio and a short note on the AI tool you think is most underrated right now and how you'd use it for a brand like Everyday Dose. Skip the cover letter. Show us how you think and what you can do.

title: Visual QA Specialist
description:
Apply here:  



The Visual QA Specialist is the last line of defense before client-facing work goes live. You'll review edits and design changes across client websites to ensure everything looks polished, intentional, and professional, not just technically correct.  



This role exists because of a specific problem: when builders interpret client feedback and make design changes, the work can be functionally correct but visually off. Alignment breaks, spacing gets inconsistent, crops look awkward, and the overall design quality drops. Your job is to catch that before it ships.  



This is QA, not design. You're not creating. You're evaluating. You need a strong design eye, obsessive attention to detail, and the ability to articulate exactly why something looks wrong, not just flag that it does.



What You'll Do  



- Review new site builds and edit requests for visual quality. Every build gets a full visual pass. Every edit that touches layout, imagery, or styling gets reviewed before it ships.

- Catch visual quality issues. Identify problems with alignment, spacing, cropping, typography, color consistency, image quality, and responsive behavior. If it looks sloppy, it doesn't ship.

- Review at every Webflow breakpoint. Check desktop, large, medium (tablet), small (mobile landscape), and tiny (mobile portrait). Catch breakpoints that silently break from desktop changes.

- Fix what you find. For minor issues (spacing, alignment, cropping), fix them directly in Webflow rather than sending work back.

- Annotate and document issues in BugHerd. When builder fixes are needed, document issues directly on the staging site with annotated screenshots and specific guidance.

- Manage visual QA tickets in Linear. Track status of reviews and revisions. Work only moves forward when it passes the visual checklist in full.

- Document visual standards. Build and maintain the visual QA checklist so "good" is written down, not assumed.

- Provide clear, actionable feedback. Feedback must be specific enough to fix without follow-up.

- Track visual quality trends. Flag recurring issues so root causes can be addressed through SOPs or training.

- Collaborate with the Implementation Leader. Report on visual quality, surface recurring issues, and help prioritize standards.



What We're Looking For



Required  



- Strong design eye. Spot spacing, alignment, typography, and color issues quickly.

- Experience with Webflow. Navigate the editor, inspect elements, and make minor fixes without a tutorial.

- Understanding of Webflow breakpoints. Know how styles cascade, and verify each breakpoint independently.

- Obsessive attention to detail. Catch small misalignments, inconsistent padding, and slightly wrong crops at mobile.

- Ability to articulate visual issues clearly. Explain why something looks off, and what to change.

- Proficiency with BugHerd. Annotate and document visual issues directly on staging sites.

- Familiarity with Linear. Manage QA ticket status as part of the review workflow.

- Strong written communication in English. QA notes must be clear enough to fix without follow-up.

- Comfort with repetitive, detail-oriented work. High volume reviews require sustained focus.

- Systematic approach to reviews. Follow a checklist, not instincts.



Nice to Have  



- Formal design training or background (graphic design, UI/UX, visual communications)

- Experience in QA or quality assurance in a web agency or SaaS environment

- Familiarity with image optimization standards (resolution, file size, aspect ratios for web)



Success in the First 6 Months  



- Visual quality issues caught in QA drop significantly. Fewer client-facing problems make it through.

- Builders start referencing the visual QA checklist before submitting work.

- You can review a standard edit in under 10 minutes with consistent accuracy.

- Feedback is specific enough that work rarely comes back a second time for the same issue.

- The team trusts your eye. When you approve something, everyone is confident it looks right.



About PushPress  



PushPress is the AI-powered gym management platform built for boutique fitness.  



We are transforming how boutique fitness owners operate and how the entire $100B global fitness industry connects, transacts, and grows. Trusted by more than 5,000 gyms and 500,000 members, PushPress processes ~$1B annually.  



We're a Series B company backed by Altos Ventures, StepStone, and Mucker Capital, and we're going all-in on AI. Our AI Assistant, AI Member Intel, and AI-powered reporting tools are already changing how gym owners and coaches operate. We believe AI should make the human side of fitness better, not replace it.  



By embedding intelligence directly into the product, we enable boutique fitness owners to make faster decisions, unlock new revenue streams, and collaborate across a connected network. As more gyms join the platform, data compounds, intelligence strengthens, and the value of the ecosystem increases for every owner.  



We are a global team of builders, operators, and fitness fanatics on a mission to level the playing field for independent fitness entrepreneurs. If you are ready to help reshape an industry, let's talk!



Why Join PushPress  

- Work fully remote from anywhere in the world.

- Inherit and improve an established operation.

- Direct leadership access with full context on company strategy.

- Above-market compensation.

- AI-native operation. Automation is core to how this team works.

- Impact that compounds. Better visual quality means better product, lower churn, and happier clients.

title: Creative Strategist & Ecommerce Manager
description:
Creative Strategist & Ecommerce Manager

Latico Leathers | Remote, International | Full-Time | $2,000–$2,500/month



About Latico Leathers

Latico Leathers is a family-run leather goods brand crafting full-grain leather handbags since 1984. We are a lean, fast-moving ecommerce team with a strong brand, a loyal customer base, and real creative standards. We know who we are. We need someone who can execute at that level.



The Role

We are hiring a Creative Strategist and Ecommerce Manager to own the day-to-day execution of our ecommerce site while acting as the internal creative gatekeeper across all marketing channels. This is a hands-on hybrid role, part ops, part creative. You will keep the site accurate and promo-ready while ensuring everything we publish looks and sounds like Latico.

If you have a strong visual eye, care about brand consistency, and can manage ecommerce operations without needing constant direction, this is for you.



What You'll Own

• Ecommerce Operations Own day-to-day Shopify execution: product pages, collections, banners, discount codes, and promotional messaging. Keep the site accurate and ready to launch at all times. Flag inventory issues that could impact campaigns before they become problems.



• Promo Calendar Execution Keep the promotional calendar on track. Coordinate deadlines, creative assets, and site updates across
, paid media, and organic. Make sure everything is set up correctly before launch, not after.



• Creative Review First-pass review on ad creative,
 drafts, website graphics, and organic content before anything goes live. You will know the brand standards well enough to catch off-brand copy, weak hooks, and visual inconsistencies without being told twice.



• Landing Page & Collection Page Builds Build and maintain landing pages in Replo and collection pages in Shopify tied to
 promos and campaigns. Strong visual judgment is essential here. These pages need to convert and look right.



• Asset & Content Management Maintain organized creative systems including photoshoot assets, creator content, and website imagery. Assets should be properly labeled, structured, and accessible across the team.



• Product Pages Manage PDPs for accuracy, imagery quality, and conversion-focused layout. Coordinate with developers on metafields, variants, and page updates.



• Reviews & Product Monitoring Approve Okendo reviews, monitor customer feedback, and surface trends in returns and product performance.



You're a Strong Fit If You

• Have 2–4 years of experience in DTC ecommerce, brand management, or creative operations

• Have a strong visual eye. You can tell when something is off-brand before anyone explains why

• Have experience or genuine interest in women's fashion, accessories, or lifestyle brands

• Are highly organized and can manage multiple moving deadlines without dropping things

• Can work independently and make judgment calls without hand-holding

• Have experience with Shopify and at least one of: Klaviyo, Figma, Canva, Replo, or Meta Ads

• Have excellent written and spoken English



Nice to Have

• Background in graphic design or visual identity

• Experience building landing pages or collection pages

• Familiarity with AI creative tools (we use them actively: Higgsfield, Flora, Claude)

• Background in paid social or
 marketing



What We Offer

• $2,000–$2,500/month depending on experience

• Fully remote

• Small team with direct access to decision-makers. No bureaucracy, no approval chains.

• A real brand with clear creative standards. Your work will be visible and it will matter.



How to Apply

Send your resume and visit 
 before applying. In your application, include two things you would improve on the site or in our marketing, and why. Keep it brief and specific. We are not looking for a portfolio deck. We are looking for judgment and attention to detail.



 your findings along with your CV to

title: Outbound Appointment Setter (BPO / High-Volume Dialer)
description:
Outbound Appointment Setter (BPO / High-Volume Dialer)

Remote | Performance Bonuses | Long-Term Role



We’re hiring experienced BPO-style outbound appointment setter to call US service business owners and connect them to our closer. Multiple openings available. Start on Monday 3/23.



This is a high-volume dialing role. Leads are provided. No research. You’ll dial one after the other.



Your job is ONLY

Live transfer the owner to our closer when available, OR

Book a 15–20 minute presentation on the closer’s calendar

You will not run demos and you will not close sales.



What You’ll Do (Daily)

Make 80–120 outbound calls/day (leads provided)

Get past gatekeepers and reach the decision maker

Live transfer to closer or book the 15–20 minute presentation

Send short follow-up messages when asked 
/text), then follow up by phone

Calls are made inside GoHighLevel (we provide the number +
 + dialer access)

Calling window: US business hours (9am–5pm local US time)



Performance Bonuses

$5 per qualified presentation attended (owner shows up)

$100 bonus if the deal closes from your appointment



Who This Is For

You should apply if you:

Have BPO / outbound call center experience

Can dial continuously and stay consistent all day

Sound confident and natural speaking with US business owners

Are comfortable handling objections and moving fast

Have reliable internet + quiet workspace + headset

BPO/call center outbound experience is strongly preferred.



Proof (Required)

In your message include:

Your BPO/call center role + outbound campaign type

Your highest daily dials and typical daily dials



How to Apply (Required)

Start your message with: APPOINTMENT

1) Upload a 30–45 second voice recording (required)

Say this exactly (sound natural, not robotic):

“Hi, this is [your name]. I know your time is tight, so I’ll be quick. If a potential customer calls after hours — say 8 PM — does someone answer, or does it go to voi
?”



2) Answer these 3 questions (required)

a) Highest calls you made in one day?

b) Last high-volume outbound job + daily volume?

c) Would 100 calls/day feel normal or stressful? Why?



No recording = not reviewed.

description:

Must be able to be with a team, and must communicate effectively and meet deadlines on time.

title: Google Ads Specialist, Fix Tracking + Generate Qualified Leads (Project-Based)
description:
We are travel and accommodation agency working with B2B clients across Europe.



We are currently running Google Ads but the account has poor tracking and unreliable conversion data. We are looking for a specialist to fix the setup and deliver measurable, real leads.



This is a short, project-based role with clear deliverables. If results are strong, this can turn into ongoing work.



CURRENT SITUATION (read carefully)

• Google Ads shows 7,000+ form submissions that are not real

• Page views and other events are counted as conversions

• Tracking is set up via GTM with custom events and likely firing incorrectly

• Campaigns have been running for months with no reliable cost per lead

If you don’t immediately understand what’s wrong here, do not apply.



YOUR JOB

1. Audit and fix conversion tracking

 • Only count real form submissions and qualified calls

 • Remove all fake or duplicate events

 • Ensure 1 real submission = 1 tracked conversion

2. Rebuild or clean campaign structure

 • Focus on high-intent B2B search terms

 • Remove low-quality traffic sources

 • Implement proper negative keywords

3. Validate tracking

 • Test with real submissions

 • Match Google Ads data with actual leads 
/CRM)

4. Launch and optimize

 • Generate first verified leads

 • Show real cost per lead



DELIVERABLES

• Clean, verified conversion tracking (no inflated numbers)

• 1–3 focused search campaigns

• Keyword + negative keyword structure

• Proof of real leads generated

• Clear cost per lead



PAYMENT

Project: $150–300

Bonus if you generate qualified leads at a strong cost



REQUIRED TO APPLY (no exceptions)

Answer these clearly and practically:

 1. A Google Ads account shows 7,000 form submissions, but the business received almost none. What is the most likely cause and how do you fix it?

 2. How do you ensure a form submission is counted only once and only when it is actually completed?

 3. When using Google Tag Manager, what would you check if a conversion event is firing too often?

 4. Would you ever track page views or button clicks as primary conversions in lead generation? Why or why not?

Keep answers short and technical.

title: Google Ads Expert – Moving Services (Multiple Locations)
description:
About Us:

We are a leading moving services company with multiple locations across Los Angeles. Our mission is to provide fast, reliable, and professional moving solutions for residential and commercial clients. We’re looking for a skilled Google Ads Expert to help us grow our reach and drive more bookings online.



Job Description:

We are seeking an experienced Google Ads Specialist to manage, optimize, and scale our advertising campaigns for multiple moving service locations. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience running high-performing campaigns for service-based businesses and be able to increase leads while maximizing ROI.



Responsibilities:



Manage Google Ads campaigns across multiple moving service locations.



Perform keyword research and develop location-specific targeting strategies.



Optimize campaigns for leads, conversions, and overall ROI.



Track and report on key performance metrics (CPC, CTR, CPA, ROAS).



A/B test ad creatives, landing pages, and audience targeting.



Collaborate with the marketing and operations teams to align campaigns with business goals.



Stay up-to-date with Google Ads trends, features, and best practices.



Requirements:



Proven experience managing Google Ads accounts, ideally for service-based businesses like moving, logistics, or home services.



Strong understanding of PPC, SEM, remarketing, and conversion tracking.



Experience with multi-location campaigns.



Ability to analyze data and implement actionable strategies.



Familiarity with Google Analytics and reporting tools.



Excellent communication skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment.



Must work in PST (Los Angeles time zone).



Google Ads certification is a plus.



Why Join Us:



Lead digital campaigns for a fast-growing moving services company.



Opportunity to scale campaigns across multiple locations.



Competitive pay based on experience and performance.



Flexible work arrangements for PST candidates.

title: Inventory & Invoice Reconciliation Specialist – Remote
description:
We are a U.S.-based wholesale food distribution company looking for someone who can improve the way our business operates.



This is not a data entry job,

We are hiring operators who think with data



We are looking for someone who can analyze operational data, identify problems, and design clean, reusable reporting systems that help management make decisions quickly.



The right person thinks like an architect, not an operator.



You should enjoy working with messy datasets and transforming them into structured systems that reveal operational and financial problems.



?



WHAT YOU WILL WORK WITH



Operational datasets such as:



* purchase orders

* vendor invoices

* inventory reports

* reconciliation reports

* margin analysis reports



Your role is to build models that quickly answer questions like:



* What went wrong?

* Where is the financial exposure?

* Which items or vendors caused the problem?



A good report should allow management to understand the issue in under two minutes.



?



hiring operators who think with data



WHO SHOULD APPLY



You will likely succeed in this role if you:



* enjoy analyzing messy datasets

* prefer building systems instead of manual lists

* design formula-driven models that update automatically

* structure reports so they are easy to audit and review

* think about financial impact, not just item counts





ASSESSMENT



To apply, complete the short analysis task below.



Download the assessment file.



The workbook contains two sheets:



Sheet 1 – Purchase Order

Sheet 2 – Vendor Bill



Your task is to analyze the differences between the two documents.



Identify:



* items missing from the vendor bill

* items added by the vendor

* price differences



Present the findings in a clear format that management can review quickly and audit.



Your submission should show how you think, structure analysis, and present operational problems.



Assessment file:







?



WORK STRUCTURE



Remote –



Schedule:

Monday – Saturday

8 hours per day



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

	1.	Download the assessment file

	2.	Complete the analysis

	3.	Submit your finished Excel workbook in .xlsx format



You may organize your workbook in any structure (single sheet or multiple sheets).



Do not include explanations in the
.

All logic should be shown inside the Excel file.



Do not delete or modify the original sheets. All sheets must be preserved so the work can be audited.

Do not convert formulas to values. The file must remain fully auditable.



Only candidates who submit the completed assessment will be reviewed.



 your submission in .xlsx format to:







Subject: Assessment



Attach the Excel file only.

Do not include any text in the body of the
.

title: IT Manager / Field Supervisor
description:
Overview

We are seeking a seasoned IT Manager / Field Supervisor to lead our Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure. This role will oversee helpdesk operations, field dispatch, and project management across a diverse client base. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments, along with a proven ability to lead teams and deliver exceptional IT support in a fast-paced MSP setting.



Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the MSP helpdesk, ensuring timely resolution of tickets and high client satisfaction

Coordinate and dispatch field technicians for on-site support and project implementations

Oversee cloud-centric IT projects including migrations, deployments, and infrastructure upgrades

Provide hands-on technical leadership in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments

Mentor and manage a team of IT professionals, fostering collaboration and growth

Monitor SLAs and KPIs, and implement process improvements for operational efficiency

Collaborate with clients to assess needs, propose cloud-first solutions, and maintain strong relationships



Required Qualifications

Minimum of 10 years in IT management, with at least 5 years in an MSP helpdesk environment leading teams

Deep technical proficiency in:

Azure AD, Office 365, Intune, SharePoint, MS Exchange Server

AWS services including EC2, S3, IAM, and VPC configurations

Windows Server (on-prem and cloud-hosted)

Google Workspace and Google Cloud infrastructure

Setup and management of Ubiquiti, Cisco, SonicWall, Synology hardware

Strong project management experience in cloud migrations and IT deployments

Proven ability to manage ticket workflows and dispatch field resources effectively

Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills

Ability to work under pressure and adapt to evolving client needs



Preferred Qualifications

Certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco CCNA

Experience with MSP tools like RMM and PSA platforms

Familiarity with cybersecurity best practices and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR)



Why Join Us

Lead a dynamic team in a growing MSP with a cloud-first approach

Competitive salary and benefits package

Access to cutting-edge technologies and ongoing professional development

Collaborative and innovative work culture



How to Apply

Please submit your resume, a cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and a short intro video. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected status.

title: Virtual Assistant for Travel Agency (Lead Generation + Client Follow-Up)
description:
Love this addition—this is a **high-conversion move** if it’s positioned correctly. I’ve woven it in so it’s clear, ethical, and not spammy ????



---



# ???? UPDATED JOB POST (WITH TRANSFER LEAD GEN ADDED)



**Title:**

???? Virtual Assistant for Travel Agency (Lead Generation + Client Follow-Up)



---



**About the Role:**

Hi! I’m Danielle, owner of *Angelina Was Here Travel*. I’m looking for a Virtual Assistant to help generate leads and follow up with potential clients so we can grow quickly.



This role is focused on **starting conversations, engaging with potential travelers, and supporting bookings**—not just admin work.



---



**What You Will Be Responsible For (Daily):**



### **1. Lead Generation (Primary Focus)**



* Find people planning trips (especially Disney, Universal, cruises, and family travel)

* Engage in
 groups and respond to travel-related posts

* Start conversations via comments and direct messages (scripts provided)

* Identify people who are **actively planning or recently booked trips**



???? Including:



* People asking for travel advice



* People feeling overwhelmed planning



* People who have **recently booked (within ~30 days)** and may benefit from support



* For recent bookings:



  * Offer helpful guidance (planning, dining, itineraries, savings monitoring)

  * Introduce the option to have a travel advisor assist or take over planning **(when allowed by supplier policies)**

  * Always lead with value and support—not sales pressure



---



### **2. Lead Follow-Up**



* Follow up with people who have already shown interest

* Keep conversations going and move leads toward booking

* Ensure no potential client is missed



---



### **3. Lead Tracking**



* Log all conversations and leads in a simple tracker (Google Sheets or CRM)

* Update status of each lead (new, contacted, interested, booked)



---



### **4. Client Support (Light)**



* Assist with sending basic information or trip details

* Support communication between Danielle and clients



---



**What This Role Is NOT:**



* Not graphic design

* Not just posting content

* Not passive social media management



???? This role requires **initiative, communication, and confidence starting conversations**



---



**Requirements:**



* Strong written English

* Comfortable messaging new people daily

* Organized and detail-oriented

* Responsive and reliable



---



**Bonus (not required but preferred):**



* Experience in travel planning or travel agencies

* Familiarity with **TESS** and/or **Outside Agents** platforms

* Sales or lead generation experience



---



**Compensation:**



* $4–$6/hour depending on experience (starting at 10 hours/week, but with potential for additional hours are we grow)

* Performance bonuses based on bookings ($10-$20 based on trip comission)



---



**Success in This Role Looks Like:**



* Starting conversations daily

* Generating new leads consistently

* Helping turn conversations into booked trips



---



**To Apply:**

Start your application with:

**“READY TO BOOK TRIPS ??”**



Then answer this question:



???? *If someone posts: “I’m overwhelmed planning a Disney trip,” how would you respond?*
